title: fix: support common email property key variants
body:
## Problem

When resolving people by email (for example, when building cohorts from CSV imports), PostHog only looked up the `email` JSON property key. Some datasets use different casing for the email property (e.g. `Email`, `EMAIL`), which caused imports / cohort lookups to miss matches. This makes CSV-based cohort resolution fragile across datasets with mixed-case property keys.


## Changes

- Updated cohort email → uuid resolution to use the exact email property key format from the CSV header:
  - posthog/api/cohort.py: CSV parser now captures the actual email column name from the CSV header and passes it through to the cohort insertion task.
  - posthog/models/cohort/cohort.py: `insert_users_by_email()` now accepts an optional `email_property_key` parameter that specifies the exact property key format to use (e.g., 'email', 'Email', 'EMAIL'). If not provided, defaults to 'email' for backward compatibility.
  - posthog/tasks/calculate_cohort.py: `calculate_cohort_from_list()` task passes the `email_column_name` from the CSV as `email_property_key` to the cohort insertion method.
  - The query uses a single filter with the exact property key format: `properties__{email_property_key}__in` to match persons.


## How did you test this code?

- Manual testing:
  - Created a small sample dataset with person property entries using `email`, `Email`, and `EMAIL` keys.
  - Ran the CSV/cohort email locally with CSV files containing different email column header formats and verified that each variant matched correctly using the exact property key format from the CSV header.
  - Confirmed the change uses a single query and returns distinct UUIDs.

👉 _Stay up-to-date with [PostHog coding conventions](https://posthog.com/docs/contribute/coding-conventions) for a smoother review._

## Publish to changelog?

no

title: fix(analytics-platform): add product and feature tags to ClickHouse queries
body:
## Problem

ClickHouse queries originating from Analytics Platform team-owned code are missing `product` and `feature` query tags. This makes it difficult to attribute query performance and resource consumption to specific products and features when monitoring ClickHouse query health.

## Changes

- `posthog/tasks/poll_query_performance.py`: Added `product=product_analytics, feature=health_check` tags to the system process monitoring query
- `posthog/api/event_definition.py`: Added `product=product_analytics, feature=query` tags to the 30-day event metrics query
- `posthog/api/element.py`: Added `product=product_analytics, feature=query` tags to the element stats and values queries
- `posthog/api/debug_ch_queries.py`: Added `product=product_analytics, feature=query` tags to all four debug ClickHouse query endpoints (hourly stats, totals, recent queries, trace profiling)
- `posthog/caching/utils.py`: Added `product=product_analytics, feature=cache_warmup` tags to `largest_teams` and `active_teams` queries

Feature/team assignment was based on the [feature ownership page](https://posthog.com/handbook/engineering/feature-ownership).

### Sibling PRs (one per team)

- Surveys: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52341
- Replay: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52338
- Product Analytics: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52350

- Data Warehouse: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52354

- Billing: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52356
- Customer Analytics: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52340
- Analytics Platform: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52339
- PostHog AI: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52342
- Infrastructure: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52344
- Feature Flags: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52347
- Data Modeling: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52348
- Workflows: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52346
- Growth: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52345
- Experiments: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/52343

## How did you test this code?

This is an agent-authored PR that adds query tags to existing ClickHouse queries. No manual testing was done beyond linting. The changes are mechanical additions of `tag_queries()` calls before `sync_execute()` calls, following the established pattern used elsewhere in the codebase.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

Not needed

## 🤖 LLM context

This PR was authored by Claude Code. The changes follow the established query tagging pattern (calling `tag_queries()` before `sync_execute()`) to ensure all Analytics Platform ClickHouse queries are properly attributed for performance monitoring.

Announcements

title: test(sharing): move initKeaTests to beforeEach
body:
<!-- mendral:session:action:01KMJ4AKNY0S58R7GH2N9TD6NJ -->
## Summary

Fixes a flaky test race condition in `SharingModal.test.tsx` identified in insight [SharingModal insight test flaky — KEA store reset race condition].

- `initKeaTests()`, `useMocks()`, and `useAvailableFeatures()` were called inside the React wrapper component bodies. This caused a race condition: async callbacks from the dashboard test (e.g., MSW API responses) could fire after the insight test had already reset the KEA store, leaving the old `sharingLogic(sharing-123)` instance unmounted and crashing with `[KEA] Can not find path "lib.components.Sharing.sharingLogic.sharing-123" in the store`.
- Moved all three calls into `beforeEach` hooks for both `describe` blocks, ensuring the KEA store is always reset before each test starts — not mid-render.

This matches the pattern used in other test files across the codebase.


---

> [!NOTE]
> Created by [Mendral](https://mendral.com). Tag @mendral-app with feedback or questions.
>
> - [View implementation session](https://app.mendral.com/sessions/action:01KMJ4AKNY0S58R7GH2N9TD6NJ)

title: fix(cdp): normalize github destination repo values
body:
## Problem

GitHub issue destinations were saving repository selections as `owner/repo`, but the runtime expected just `repo`. That caused duplicated owner paths and 404s when creating issues. Organization installs were also linking to the wrong GitHub settings URL.

Related ticket: https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/53265

## Changes

- store GitHub repository selections canonically while still showing `owner/repo` in the picker
- keep legacy saved `owner/repo` values working in both execution paths
- send organization installs to the correct GitHub settings URL
- add focused regression coverage for repository normalization and installation URL generation

## How did you test this code?

- `pytest posthog/models/test/test_integration_model.py -k "create_issue_accepts or list_repositories"`
- `pnpm --filter=@posthog/frontend exec jest --runInBand --watchman=false src/lib/integrations/githubUtils.test.ts`
- added `nodejs/src/cdp/templates/_destinations/github/github.template.test.ts`
- I could not run the new nodejs Jest test locally because the available Node runtime does not match the compiled `re2` native module in this environment

## Publish to changelog?

no

## Docs update

No docs update needed.

title: fix(feature-flags): preserve new flag draft when switching browser tabs
body:
## Problem

Closes #50537

When creating a new feature flag, switching to another browser tab and returning causes all unsaved form data to be lost. This happens because the browser's `visibilitychange` event triggers kea disposables to resume, which causes kea-router to fire a PUSH navigation. The `urlToAction` handler then calls `loadFeatureFlag()`, resetting the form back to defaults.

## Changes

- In `featureFlagLogic.ts`, expanded the existing draft-preservation guard in `urlToAction` to detect any in-progress edits (key, name, tags, multivariate variants, or payloads) before allowing a reload on PUSH navigation for new flags
- Added two unit tests covering both paths: draft preserved on URL push, and reload still triggered when no edits exist

## How did you test this code?

- Added automated tests in `featureFlagLogic.test.ts` (64/64 passing)

## Publish to changelog?

no

title: feat(feature-flags): add page leave warning for unsaved changes
body:
## Problem

Closes #5187

Users can easily lose their work when navigating away from the Feature Flags edit page without saving. There is no warning or confirmation dialog, and changes are silently discarded.

## Changes

Added a `beforeunload` event listener that triggers the browser's native "Leave site?" confirmation dialog whenever the feature flag form has unsaved changes. The warning is driven by the kea-forms `featureFlagChanged` value, which automatically tracks whether the form has been modified since it was last loaded or saved.

The handler is added in both components that render the feature flag edit form:
- `FeatureFlag.tsx` -- the legacy edit UI
- `FeatureFlagForm.tsx` -- the v2 form UI (behind the `FEATURE_FLAGS_V2` feature flag)

When `featureFlagChanged` becomes `true`, the `beforeunload` listener is attached. When it becomes `false` (e.g., after saving), the listener is removed. This prevents false positives after a successful save.

## How did you test this code?

I am an agent and have not tested this manually. The implementation follows the same `beforeunload` pattern already used in other parts of the PostHog codebase (e.g., `ResultsNotificationBanner.tsx`). The `featureFlagChanged` selector is automatically provided by kea-forms and is already used in similar forms throughout the codebase (e.g., `subscriptionChanged`, `configurationChanged`, `userDetailsChanged`).

To test manually:
- Navigate to a feature flag edit page
- Make any change (toggle, edit key, modify release conditions)
- Try to close the tab or navigate away -- the browser should show a "Leave site?" warning
- Save the changes, then try navigating away -- no warning should appear

## Publish to changelog?

no

## Docs update

No docs update needed.

## 🤖 LLM context

This PR was authored by an LLM agent (Claude). The change is minimal and self-contained, adding a standard browser `beforeunload` warning pattern.

title: feat(batch-exports): track event when backfill is created
body:
## Changes

Adds a `batch export backfill created` PostHog analytics event when a user starts a batch export backfill via the API. This enables tracking usage patterns for the backfill feature.

**Event properties captured:**
- `batch_export_id` — the batch export being backfilled
- `batch_export_name` — human-readable name
- `destination_type` — e.g. S3, BigQuery, etc.
- `interval` — the batch export interval (hour, day, etc.)
- `backfill_id` — the newly created backfill ID
- `start_at` / `end_at` — the requested backfill date range

The event is fired after the backfill is successfully created (Temporal workflow started), so failed validation or missing batch exports won't produce events.

## Test plan

- [x] Added test that mocks `report_user_action` and verifies the event name and all properties

title: feat(replay): route eligible filters through sessions v3 for faster queries

labels.name: skip-inkeep-docs
labels.description: Use this label to skip an Inkeep docs PR in posthog.com

body:
## Problem

Session Replay list queries currently filter `$host`, `$current_url`, `email`, and event names by subquerying the **events table** — scanning every event row in the date range, extracting JSON properties, and grouping by session. This is expensive, especially for the common "filter out internal/test users" case (`filter_test_accounts=true`), which produces negative-operator filters like `$host not_regex localhost` and `email not_icontains @company.com`.

PR #50037 added pre-aggregated `hosts` and `emails` array columns to `raw_sessions_v3` with bloom filter indexes. The table also already had `urls` and `event_names` array columns. All four are bloom-indexed and contain exactly the data needed for these filters, at session-level granularity (1 row per session vs dozens-to-hundreds of event rows per session).

## Changes

When the `replay-filters-via-sessions-v3` feature flag is enabled, eligible property and event name filters are intercepted in `SessionRecordingListFromQuery.__init__` and routed through a `raw_sessions_v3` subquery instead of the events table:

| Filter | Type | Sessions v3 column |
|--------|------|--------------------|
| `$host` | event | `hosts` |
| `$current_url` | event | `urls` |
| `email` | person | `emails` |
| simple event entity (no property filters) | entity | `event_names` |

Operator translation maps property operators (exact, is_not, icontains, not_icontains, regex, not_regex, is_set, is_not_set) to array predicates (has, arrayExists + ilike/match, etc.).

Since `raw_sessions_v3` is an AggregatingMergeTree with multiple parts per session, array columns are aggregated via `arrayDistinct(arrayFlatten(groupArray(col)))` in HAVING before filtering — matching the pattern used by the sessions v3 HogQL schema.

Also adds the `event_names` field to the `RAW_SESSIONS_FIELDS` HogQL schema (the column existed in ClickHouse but wasn't registered).

## How did you test this code?

9 new tests in `TestSessionRecordingsListFromQuerySessionsV3` covering:
- `$host` filter (positive + negative operators)
- `email` filter (negative operator)
- `$current_url` filter (positive operator)
- Simple event entity via `event_names`
- Event entity with properties still goes through events subquery
- `filter_test_accounts=True` routes `$host` and `email` through sessions v3
- Feature flag off → existing behavior unchanged
- Mixed filters: sessions v3 filters + other event properties coexist

All 205 existing tests pass with no regressions.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

No docs changes needed.

## Problem

Users need to enable cookieless tracking in **two places**: the SDK config AND the project settings. This is confusing and causes support tickets when users enable it in the SDK but forget the project setting.

Additionally, the stateless mode is not used by any projects and adds unnecessary complexity.

## Changes

- **Sunset stateless mode** — all cookieless events are now processed as stateful
- **Remove the project setting requirement** — cookieless tracking now works when enabled in the SDK only, no additional project configuration needed

|------|--------|
| Node.js ingestion | Always process cookieless events as stateful, ignore team setting |
| Rust (feature-flags) | Always use stateful mode |
| Backend API | Remove `cookieless_server_hash_mode` from exposed fields |
| Frontend | Remove the settings UI entirely |

title: feat(cdp): add metrics to scale hog workers
body: ### Summary The cdp-cyclotron-worker's main job is making external HTTP requests (webhooks, destinations). It's I/O bound — most time is spent waiting on responses, not doing CPU work. Our current scaling metrics don't capture this well: - CPU: stays low during I/O wait, so the HPA thinks the worker is idle even when it's fully saturated with pending HTTP requests - Batch utilization: measures how full the dequeued batch is (demand), not how busy the worker is (capacity). This causes a rollercoaster scaling pattern: HPA sees low CPU + stale batch util → doesn't scale → work piles up → eventually scales too late → new pods are idle → scales back down → repeat. ### Changes This PR adds two Prometheus metrics that directly measure HTTP-level saturation: `cdp_http_inflight_requests` — number of external fetch() calls currently in flight. This is the primary scaling signal: it smoothly reflects how much work a pod is handling. `cdp_http_pending_requests` — number of requests queued inside undici waiting for a free connection slot (via diagnostics_channel). Non-zero means the connection pool (EXTERNAL_REQUEST_CONNECTIONS) is fully saturated — a strong signal to scale out. ### How to use for scaling (potentially) Primary signal: cdp_http_inflight_requests — scale when the average per-pod value exceeds a threshold relative to batch size. Since all requests in a batch fire via Promise.all(), this metric will range from 0 to CONSUMER_BATCH_SIZE (1000 in prod). A good starting threshold might be ~700 (70% of batch size). Supplementary signal: cdp_http_pending_requests — persistently > 0 means at least one destination's per-origin connection pool (80 sockets per origin in prod) is full and requests to it are queuing. Use a 5-minute scale-down stabilization window to prevent the current oscillation (fiddle around with it to find good fit). ### Connection pool vs batch size Important: EXTERNAL_REQUEST_CONNECTIONS is a per-origin limit, not a global cap. In us-prod it's set to 80 — meaning undici opens at most 80 concurrent sockets to each distinct destination host. Since the worker hits many different origins (Slack, Zapier, customer webhooks, etc.), the total number of concurrent sockets across all origins can be much higher than 80. Right now processInvocations() fires all 1000 fetches via Promise.all(). If those requests are spread across many origins, they'll mostly all be on the wire simultaneously. If many hit the same origin (e.g. a popular webhook like Slack), only 80 go on the wire and the rest queue per-origin inside undici. This means the batch size is effectively the concurrency limit, not the connection pool. With batch size 1000, a pod can have up to 1000 HTTP requests in flight across all destinations. ### Should we lower the batch size? If we dequeue 1000 jobs but a pod is saturated, those jobs are stuck in this pod while other pods might be idle. A smaller batch size means work gets distributed more evenly — especially important for autoscaling since new pods can pick up work faster. The tradeoff is more frequent dequeue round-trips to Kafka/Postgres. Best approach: deploy these metrics first, observe the actual inflight/pending numbers in Grafana, then tune batch size based on real data.

title: chore(workflows): rework billing
body:
### Problem: 

Workflow billing counted all function/function_email/function_sms/function_push actions as billable, even when they use free internal templates (e.g. "Capture event", "Set person properties"). The billable_action_types field was precomputed at save time. It makes changes hard to make certain actions (like posthog actions) free. 

### Changes

Use template.free as the source of truth at runtime. At the quota gate, dynamically resolve templates and only block flows that contain at least one non-free billable action. At per-invocation billing, skip billable_invocation metrics for free template actions. Drop the precomputed billable_action_types field entirely.

- Adds free?: boolean to HogFunctionType and propagates it from the template in buildHogFunction()
- Rewrites quota limiting to dynamically compute billable action types via HogFunctionTemplateManagerService
- Removes billable_action_types from the Django model, API serializers, admin, Node schema, and HogFlow manager
- Deletes the backfill management command and its tests
- Django migration to drop the billable_action_types column

title: Add text card management tools for dashboards
body:
## Problem

Users need the ability to create, update, and delete text cards on dashboards through the MCP interface. Text cards allow users to add custom markdown content to dashboards for documentation, annotations, and context.

## Changes

Added three new dashboard text card management tools:

1. **dashboard-text-card-create**: Creates a new text card on a dashboard with markdown body content and optional color customization
2. **dashboard-text-card-update**: Updates an existing text card's content and color
3. **dashboard-text-card-delete**: Removes a text card from a dashboard

### Implementation Details

- Added `CreateTextCardSchema`, `UpdateTextCardSchema`, and `DeleteTextCardSchema` to `services/mcp/src/schema/dashboards.ts`
- Implemented handler functions in new files:
  - `services/mcp/src/tools/dashboards/createTextCard.ts`
  - `services/mcp/src/tools/dashboards/updateTextCard.ts`
  - `services/mcp/src/tools/dashboards/deleteTextCard.ts`
- Updated tool definitions in `services/mcp/schema/tool-definitions.json` with metadata for the three new tools
- Updated `services/mcp/src/schema/tool-inputs.ts` to export the new schemas
- Updated `services/mcp/src/tools/index.ts` to import and register the new tools
- Applied code formatting standardization (single quotes to double quotes, consistent spacing)

### Testing

Added comprehensive integration tests in `services/mcp/tests/tools/textCards.integration.test.ts` covering:
- Creating text cards with markdown content
- Creating text cards with color customization
- Updating text card content
- Updating text card color
- Deleting text cards
- Verifying dashboard URL generation

## How did you test this code?

Added integration tests that verify:
- Text card creation with markdown body and optional color
- Text card updates for both content and color
- Text card deletion
- Proper dashboard URL generation in responses
- Error handling for invalid inputs

The tests use the existing test infrastructure with proper resource cleanup.

## Publish to changelog?

Yes - New feature adding text card management capabilities to dashboard tools.

title: feat(insights): add filter to show only insights not on any dashboard
body:
## Problem

Users accumulate insights over time that aren't linked to any dashboard, making it impossible to distinguish "active" insights from unused ones. There's currently no way to filter for insights that are orphaned (so not included in any dashboard)

Closes #26621
Closes #17176

## Changes

Added a new **"Unused"** quick-filter button to the saved insights list. When toggled on, only insights that are not part of any dashboard are shown. This surfaces orphaned insights and makes it easy to clean them up or understand what's actively in use.

**Frontend**
- Added `notOnDashboard` to `SavedInsightFilters` interface and `cleanFilters` in `savedInsightsLogic.ts`
- Passes `not_on_dashboard=true` query param to the API when the filter is active
- Added an `IconCircleDashed` "Unused" toggle button in `SavedInsightsFilters.tsx`, consistent with the existing "Favorites" quick-filter pattern

**Backend**
- The insights API (`/api/environments/:id/insights/`) already supported a `not_on_dashboard` query param, which excludes any insight that appears in a `DashboardTile`. The frontend now exposes this capability.

![Cap 2026-03-09 at 20 01 49](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1dc383ed-e8dc-48aa-9cec-03e2aa61d074)

## How did you test this code?

I’ve added a unit test for the backend stuff at `posthog/api/test/test_insight.py`

I also tested manually by:
1. Opening the saved insights page
2. Creating insights, some added to dashboards, some not
3. Toggling the "Unused" filter and verifying only insights with no dashboard association are returned
4. Verifying the filter can be combined with other quick filters (search, type, created-by)
5. Verifying the filter state is reflected in the URL so it is shareable/bookmarkable


## Publish to changelog?

No?

title: feat: ReviewHog - Automated PR reviews through spawning tens of coding agents inside sandboxes
body: ## Problem - Product autonomy: We trust coding agents to generate a correct PR and merge it right away, but we need am addional layer on top to confirm the generated PR is actually correct - Ship faster: We already work on auto-approving PRs by agents ([PR](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/49727)), but these PRs can go inside without a proper review ## Changes - Now we can review PRs paranoidly automatically with pretty good results (got amazing feedback already, collecting more) ## How it works If we simplify, it spawns lots of coding sandboxes with the correct repo/branch and then does: - Collecting PR context with all the comments/conversations/bots, to not repeat already highlighted issues - Chunking (if large PRs) - Context on each chunk to speed up next runs - Multiple code review passes - Pass 1: Logic & Correctness - Pass 2: Contracts & Security - Pass 3: Performance & Reliability - Deduplication - Validation of all the found issues with another agent - Report ## How did you test this code? 👉 _Stay up-to-date with [PostHog coding conventions](https://posthog.com/docs/contribute/coding-conventions) for a smoother review._ ## Publish to changelog?

title: feat(workflows): emit email engagement events as standard PostHog events
body:
## Summary

- Enables email engagement events (sent, opened, clicked, bounced, blocked, spam, unsubscribed) to be captured as standard PostHog events (`$messaging_email_*`) alongside existing Workflow metrics
- Users can now build insights, funnels, and dashboards from messaging engagement data, and trigger workflows based on message interactions
- Extends the email tracking code to embed `distinct_id` so asynchronous events (opens, clicks, bounces from SES webhooks/pixels) are attributed to the correct person
- Adds `queuePostHogEvent()` to `HogFunctionMonitoringService` for direct event capture outside the invocation result flow
- Fixes pixel/redirect handlers to parse the combined `ph_id` URL parameter (previously only read legacy separate `ph_fn_id`/`ph_inv_id` params, which didn't match the URL format generated for emails)
- Maintains backwards compatibility with old tracking code format (without `distinct_id`)

## Event names emitted

| Metric name | PostHog event |
|---|---|
| `email_sent` | `$messaging_email_sent` |
| `email_failed` | `$messaging_email_failed` |
| `email_opened` | `$messaging_email_opened` |
| `email_link_clicked` | `$messaging_email_link_clicked` |
| `email_bounced` | `$messaging_email_bounced` |
| `email_blocked` | `$messaging_email_blocked` |
| `email_spam` | `$messaging_email_spam` |
| `email_unsubscribed` | `$messaging_email_unsubscribed` |

## Event properties

All events include:
- `$workflow_id` — the workflow or function ID
- `$messaging_source` — how the event was detected (`direct` for pixel/redirect, `ses` for SES webhooks)

Additional properties per event type:
- `$email_to` — recipient email (from SES webhooks and send path)
- `$email_subject` — email subject (from send path)
- `$link_url` — clicked link URL (for click events)

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for tracking code generation/parsing with distinct_id (new)
- [x] Unit tests for SES webhook handler returning distinctId and properties (updated)
- [x] Integration tests for email tracking pixel with combined ph_id format (updated)
- [x] Integration tests for email tracking redirect with combined ph_id format (updated)
- [x] Integration tests for legacy ph_fn_id/ph_inv_id backwards compatibility (new)
- [x] Integration tests for $messaging_email_sent/failed capture (new)
- [ ] Manual test: trigger a workflow email, verify events appear in PostHog event stream

title: feat(storage): swap objectstorage to seaweedfs compatibly
body:
## Summary
- switch objectstorage service in base compose from MinIO to SeaweedFS while preserving the existing objectstorage:19000 contract
- add SeaweedFS S3 identity config that accepts both legacy MinIO-style credentials and any/any
- bootstrap required buckets on startup (posthog, ducklake-dev, ai-blobs in main stack; capture, posthog in rust stack)
- keep hobby objectstorage on MinIO as a transitional override so existing hobby migration scripts still have a MinIO source

## Why
This unblocks migration away from MinIO in dev/CI with minimal code churn and without forcing endpoint/credential updates across tests and services in the same PR.

## Validation
- docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml config
- docker compose -f docker-compose.hobby.yml config
- docker compose -f rust/docker-compose.yml config
- brought up objectstorage in dev and rust stacks and verified S3 bucket listing on http://127.0.0.1:19000 using both credential pairs:
  - object_storage_root_user/object_storage_root_password
  - any/any

title: feat:  update dashboard template to include both pageview and screen events
body:
## Problem

The original dashboard template only tracked `$pageview` events, which doesn't account for mobile app users who generate `$screen` events instead. This limits the usefulness of the template for products that support both web and mobile platforms.

## Changes

![CleanShot 2026-03-05 at 17 05 22](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/54e95ceb-4d63-4d60-a570-b9b7033d53fe)

Updated the default dashboard template to track both `$pageview` (web) and `$screen` (mobile) events across multiple insights:

some insights support combined events now so used that, others we create an action that matches pageview or screen and use that, others are web only

- **DAU (Daily Active Users)**: Changed from single `$pageview` event to a `GroupNode` combining both `$pageview` and `$screen` events with OR logic
- **WAU (Weekly Active Users)**: Applied the same change to track both event types
- **Retention**: Updated `targetEntity` and `returningEntity` to use HogQL filter `event in ('$pageview', '$screen')` instead of single event type
- **Growth Accounting (Lifecycle)**: Updated to track both event types
- **Descriptions**: Updated dashboard card descriptions to clarify they now track "page or screen views" instead of just pageviews
- **Web-only insights**: Added "Web only" clarification to "Referring domain" and "Pageview funnel by browser" descriptions since these are inherently web-specific

## How did you test this code?

created a dashboard locally and peeped at it with my eyes

## Publish to changelog?

Yes - this improves the default dashboard template to better support multi-platform products.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EcgqZoJ6cftMFV3Hv5m8of

title: feat: Connect Stripe via OAuth in data warehouse
body:
- **Backend**: Implemented `StripeIntegration` class with OAuth token management and Stripe Secret Store integration
- **Data Sources**: Updated Stripe source to support both OAuth and API key authentication methods
- **OAuth Flow**: Added Stripe OAuth configuration with proper token exchange and refresh logic
- **PostHog Token Management**: Automatically creates and manages PostHog OAuth tokens for the Stripe app to access user data

Users can now connect their Stripe account through OAuth instead of manually creating API keys. The system automatically handles token refresh and stores PostHog access credentials in Stripe's Secret Store for seamless integration.

![image.png](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/f4ebb0de-8f29-4aa3-b79d-b21637a90f31.png)

## How did you test this code?

Added comprehensive unit tests covering:
- Stripe OAuth token exchange and account name resolution
- PostHog OAuth token creation and Stripe Secret Store management  
- Token cleanup and revocation flows
- Error handling for missing configurations
- Both OAuth and API key authentication paths in the data source

The tests verify the complete OAuth flow, token management, and fallback to existing API key functionality when the feature flag is disabled.

## Publish to changelog?

Yes - this significantly improves the Stripe integration user experience by removing manual API key setup friction.

title: feat(clickhouse): add merge failures custom metric
body:
## Problem

Add visibility into ClickHouse merge failures. When background merges fail repeatedly, it can cascade > consumes CPU > can impact ingestion.

## Changes

Adds two custom metrics querying `system.part_log`:

- **`ClickHouseCustomMetric_MergeFailures15m`** — total failed merges on the node in the last 15 minutes
- **`ClickHouseCustomMetric_MergeRetriesMaxPerTable15m`** — highest failed-merge count among all table/partition combinations on the node

Also fixes the CI integration test failure:
- Added `<part_log>` to Docker ClickHouse `config.xml` so `system.part_log` exists on fresh instances (matching existing `<crash_log>` pattern)
- Made `custom_metrics_merge_failures` opt-in via `include_merge_failures` parameter (matching `include_server_crash` / `include_table_sizes` pattern)
- Renumbered migration `0218` → `0220` after rebase conflict with master

## How did you test this code?

- Verified both queries return correct results against production ClickHouse
- Confirmed `event_type = 'MergeParts'` filter is correct
- Pattern matches existing custom metrics (replication queue, events recent lag, server crash, table sizes)

## Publish to changelog?

No

title: fix(phai): acknowledge limitations instead of suggesting UI workarounds

labels.name: evals-ready
labels.description: Whether to run AI evals on this PR.

body:
## Problem

When users ask the AI assistant to perform actions it cannot do (e.g., saving insights, exporting data, modifying saved resources), the assistant would sometimes suggest that the user perform these actions manually through the PostHog UI. This is a poor user experience — the assistant should honestly acknowledge its limitations rather than deflecting to UI instructions.

## Changes

- Added a prompt instruction in the root agent telling it to directly state when it cannot perform an action, rather than suggesting UI workarounds
- Added an eval suite (`eval_root_limitations`) with an LLM-as-judge scorer that tests three scenarios:
  - Saving a SQL insight
  - Saving a trends query to a dashboard
  - Exporting data as CSV

## How did you test this code?

The eval suite itself validates the behavior using `CapabilityAcknowledgment`, an LLM-as-judge scorer that checks whether the assistant provides an honest acknowledgment vs. suggesting UI actions.

## Publish to changelog?

no

title: feat(workflows): Store, manage and use push notification device tokens
body: ## Problem We need to securely collect device tokens from enduser phones and we want to enable a many-to-many relationship between tokens and users (eg. for one user with multiple phones, or multiple accounts on one phone) ## Changes ### Database * New `PushSubscription` table with ```javascript { id, distinct_id, // used to map users and tokens token, // device token we need to send notifications token_hash, platform, // android or ios provider, // FCM (firebase/google), APNS (apple) - iOS can also receive notifications through FCM, but notifications need to be handled differently firebase_app_id, // needed to support multiple android apps in one posthog account created_at, updated_at, last_successfully_used_at, is_active, // used to disable eg. invalid tokens disabled_reason, team } ``` ### Push subscription management * New endpoint to create PushSubscriptions * New "Firebase Push Notification" destination that sends push notifications * Tokens are looked up in the backend: (`push-subscriptions-manager.service.ts`) * first by looking at the original distinct_id for the triggering event * If not found: * get distinct_ids associated with that original distinct_id from persons-DB * look up subscriptions for all those distinct_ids * Logic for updating invalid subscriptions - applied after each (attempted) use: (`push-subscriptions-manager.service.ts`) * 404 -> token wasn't registered, deactivate * 400 `INVALID_ARGUMENT` -> token invalid, deactivate Token lifecycle docs: https://firebase.google.com/docs/cloud-messaging/manage-tokens ### Firebase Notification Destination: `firebase_push.template.ts` * Takes a distinctId that is resolved to a push notification subscription Note that this PR currently includes the migration and the code that uses it, which breaks the CI pipeline. I will split the migration into a separate PR after review. Migration-only PR (needs to be merged first): https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/46358 ## How did you test this code? Automated and manual tests ## Accepted risks It's currently possible that someone who gets access to the posthog API key of a customer can sign themselves up for push notifications without installing the customer's app. This is a risk we accept for now, as it only affects customers with a private app and our biggest competitors also do not guard against it

title: fix(notifications): match SSE reconnect strategy to /events endpoint
body:
## Problem

The notifications SSE handler uses a 15s heartbeat to keep connections alive, but Contour's 120s `response` timeout measures total connection duration (not idle time), so the heartbeat is ineffective. Connections are killed at exactly 120s regardless. The current `onError` throws to stop retries, which permanently drops users to polling instead of reconnecting.

The `/events` SSE endpoint handles this correctly — it lets `fetchEventSource` silently reconnect on disconnection, providing a seamless experience despite the 120s proxy timeout.

## Changes

- Remove the 15s heartbeat ticker from the notifications SSE Go handler
- Change the frontend `onError` to not throw, allowing `fetchEventSource` to silently reconnect (matching the `/events` endpoint pattern)
- Remove the `.catch()` handler that was only needed because of the throw

## How did you test this code?

- No automated tests added
- Verified via Contour access logs in Grafana that notifications connections consistently last exactly 120000ms due to the response timeout, confirming the heartbeat has no effect

## Publish to changelog?

no

Updates `sqlparse` from 0.5.0 to 0.5.4
Changelog

Sourced from sqlparse's changelog.

Release 0.5.4 (Nov 28, 2025)

Enhancements

  • Add support for Python 3.14.
  • Add type annotations to top-level API functions and include py.typed marker for PEP 561 compliance, enabling type checking with mypy and other tools (issue756).
  • Add pre-commit hook support. sqlparse can now be used as a pre-commit hook to automatically format SQL files. The CLI now supports multiple files and an --in-place flag for in-place editing (issue537).
  • Add ATTACH and DETACH to PostgreSQL keywords (pr808).
  • Add INTERSECT to close keywords in WHERE clause (pr820).
  • Support REGEXP BINARY comparison operator (pr817).

Bug Fixes

  • Add additional protection against denial of service attacks when parsing very large lists of tuples. This enhances the existing recursion protections with configurable limits for token processing to prevent DoS through algorithmic complexity attacks. The new limits (MAX_GROUPING_DEPTH=100, MAX_GROUPING_TOKENS=10000) can be adjusted or disabled (by setting to None) if needed for legitimate large SQL statements.
  • Remove shebang from cli.py and remove executable flag (pr818).
  • Fix strip_comments not removing all comments when input contains only comments (issue801, pr803 by stropysh).
  • Fix splitting statements with IF EXISTS/IF NOT EXISTS inside BEGIN...END blocks (issue812).
  • Fix splitting on semicolons inside BEGIN...END blocks (issue809).

Release 0.5.3 (Dez 10, 2024)

Bug Fixes

  • This version introduces a more generalized handling of potential denial of service attack (DOS) due to recursion errors for deeply nested statements. Brought up and fixed by @​living180. Thanks a lot!

Release 0.5.2 (Nov 14, 2024)

Bug Fixes

  • EXTENSION is now recognized as a keyword (issue785).
  • SQL hints are not removed when removing comments (issue262, by skryzh).

Updates `black` from 24.3.0 to 26.3.1
Release notes

Sourced from black's releases.

26.3.1

Stable style

  • Prevent Jupyter notebook magic masking collisions from corrupting cells by using exact-length placeholders for short magics and aborting if a placeholder can no longer be unmasked safely (#5038)

Configuration

  • Always hash cache filename components derived from --python-cell-magics so custom magic names cannot affect cache paths (#5038)

Blackd

  • Disable browser-originated requests by default, add configurable origin allowlisting and request body limits, and bound executor submissions to improve backpressure (#5039)

26.3.0

Stable style

  • Don't double-decode input, causing non-UTF-8 files to be corrupted (#4964)
  • Fix crash on standalone comment in lambda default arguments (#4993)
  • Preserve parentheses when # type: ignore comments would be merged with other comments on the same line, preventing AST equivalence failures (#4888)

Preview style

  • Fix bug where if guards in case blocks were incorrectly split when the pattern had a trailing comma (#4884)
  • Fix string_processing crashing on unassigned long string literals with trailing commas (one-item tuples) (#4929)
  • Simplify implementation of the power operator "hugging" logic (#4918)

Packaging

  • Fix shutdown errors in PyInstaller builds on macOS by disabling multiprocessing in frozen environments (#4930)

Performance

  • Introduce winloop for windows as an alternative to uvloop (#4996)
  • Remove deprecated function uvloop.install() in favor of uvloop.new_event_loop() (#4996)
  • Rename maybe_install_uvloop function to maybe_use_uvloop to simplify loop installation and creation of either a uvloop/winloop evenloop or default eventloop (#4996)

Updates `cbor2` from 5.8.0 to 5.9.0
Release notes

Sourced from cbor2's releases.

5.9.0

  • Added the max_depth decoder parameter to limit the maximum allowed nesting level of containers, with a default value of 400 levels (CVE-2026-26209)
  • Changed the default read_size from 4096 to 1 for backwards compatibility. The buffered reads introduced in 5.8.0 could cause issues when code needs to access the stream position after decoding. Users can opt-in to faster decoding by passing read_size=4096 when they don't need to access the stream directly after decoding. Added a direct read path for read_size=1 to avoid buffer management overhead. (#275; PR by @​andreer)
  • Fixed C encoder not respecting string referencing when encoding string-type datetimes (tag 0) (#254)
  • Fixed a missed check for an exception in the C implementation of CBOREncoder.encode_shared() (#287)
  • Fixed two reference/memory leaks in the C extension's long string decoder (#290 PR by @​killiancowan82)
  • Fixed C decoder ignoring the str_errors setting when decoding strings, and improved string decoding performance by using stack allocation for small strings and eliminating unnecessary conditionals. Benchmarks show 9-17% faster deserialization. (#255; PR by @​andreer)

title: feat(llma): add ID-based summarization and enable MCP tool
body:
## Problem

The summarization endpoint requires callers to pass the full trace/event data blob. This makes it unusable from MCP tools, where agents only have trace or generation IDs from previous exploration (clusters, traces list, etc).

## Changes

**Backend** (`products/llm_analytics/backend/api/summarization.py`):
- Extend `SummarizeRequestSerializer` with optional `trace_id`, `generation_id`, `date_from`, `date_to` fields
- Add cross-field validation: provide either `data` or one of the IDs, not both
- `summarize_type` is now optional — inferred from which ID field is provided
- Add `_fetch_trace_data()` — uses `TraceQueryRunner` to fetch trace, then `llm_trace_to_formatter_format` to convert to formatter input
- Add `_fetch_generation_data()` — HogQL query to fetch a single `$ai_generation` event by UUID
- Update `create()` to use fetch methods when IDs are provided, falling back to existing `data` path

**MCP** (`products/llm_analytics/mcp/tools.yaml`):
- Enable `llm-analytics-summarization-create` with description focused on the ID-based usage

Fully backward compatible — existing callers using the `data` field are unaffected.

## How did you test this code?

Agent-authored PR. Backend logic changes need test coverage — the existing tests in `test_summarization.py` test the data-blob path. New tests should cover:
- [ ] `trace_id` path: fetches trace and returns summary
- [ ] `generation_id` path: fetches generation and returns summary
- [ ] Validation: rejects when both `data` and `trace_id` provided
- [ ] Validation: rejects when neither `data` nor ID provided
- [ ] 404 when trace/generation not found in date range

## Publish to changelog?

No

## 🤖 LLM context

Agent-authored. This is a companion to #53107 (clustering tools) and #53113 (sentiment tool). The ID-based summarization completes the MCP workflow: explore clusters → find traces → summarize them — all without the agent needing to pass large data blobs.

Key design decision: extend the existing endpoint rather than creating a new one, so the same MCP tool operation ID works for both usage patterns.

title: feat(llma): enable sentiment analysis MCP tool
body:
## Problem

MCP users exploring LLM traces and clusters have no way to analyze user message sentiment. The sentiment API exists and is production-ready (rate-limited, cached, access-controlled), but the MCP tool is disabled.

## Changes

Enable `llm-analytics-sentiment-create` in `products/llm_analytics/mcp/tools.yaml` with proper scopes and annotations.

The tool accepts a list of trace or generation IDs and returns sentiment classification (positive/neutral/negative) with confidence scores and per-message breakdowns. Results are cached server-side.

No backend changes — just YAML config.

## How did you test this code?

Agent-authored PR. YAML-only change, no logic to test. Verified lint/format hooks pass.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## 🤖 LLM context

Agent-authored. This is a companion to #53107 (clustering read tools). The sentiment tool completes a natural MCP workflow: explore clusters → find interesting traces → analyze sentiment.

title: feat(linkedin_ads): add cost_in_local_currency to LinkedIn Ads connector
body:
Based on #52945

Resolves #52911

## Problem

The LinkedIn Ads data warehouse connector syncs `campaign_stats` and `campaign_group_stats` tables that include `cost_in_usd` and `conversion_value_in_local_currency`, but are missing `cost_in_local_currency`. The LinkedIn Marketing API's `/rest/adAnalytics` endpoint already returns `costInLocalCurrency` in the same response payload — it just isn't being extracted.

This means users who need cost data in their local currency (for non-USD accounts) have no way to get it from the connector without this field.

## Changes

- Add `costInLocalCurrency` to `FLOAT_FIELDS` in `schemas.py` so API values are converted to float
- Add `costInLocalCurrency` to `field_names` for both `CampaignStats` and `CampaignGroupStats` resource schemas
- Parametrize `test_flatten_float_fields` to cover all three float fields (`costInUsd`, `costInLocalCurrency`, `conversionValueInLocalCurrency`)
- Add `cost_in_local_currency` column to the marketing analytics test CSV and column schema

No changes needed to `linkedin_ads.py` (flattening logic) or `client.py` (API field list) — both derive behavior from the schema definitions automatically. DLT handles schema evolution for the new nullable column on next sync.

## How did you test this code?

- LinkedIn Ads unit tests cover the new parametrized float field conversion
- Existing `test_adapters.py` column schema updated to include the new field

## Publish to changelog?

No

title: fix: Use CDP_PRODUCER for hogflow scheduler Kafka producer
body:
## Problem

The hogflow scheduler was using the default Kafka producer which reads from `KAFKA_PRODUCER_METADATA_BROKER_LIST` (pointing at MSK events). The `cdp_batch_hogflow_requests` topic only exists on WarpStream Cyclotron, so all dispatch attempts failed with `unknown topic or partition`.

## Changes

Switch from `KafkaProducerWrapper.create(rack)` to `KafkaProducerWrapper.create(rack, 'CDP_PRODUCER')` so the scheduler reads from `KAFKA_CDP_PRODUCER_METADATA_BROKER_LIST` which already points at WarpStream Cyclotron via the shared CDP config. This matches how the Cyclotron job queue and cohort membership consumer create their producers.

## How did you test this code?

Verified in dev that the scheduler was failing with `unknown topic or partition` on `cdp_batch_hogflow_requests`. Confirmed the topic exists on WarpStream Cyclotron via Kafbat UI. The `CDP_PRODUCER` env vars are already configured in `shared/cdp/common.dev.yaml`.

## Publish to changelog?

no

## Docs update

No docs update needed.

title: fix: prevent race condition in SelectExistingFeatureFlagModal test
body:
## Problem
The test was calling `openSelectExistingFeatureFlagModal()` immediately after mounting the logic, but `teamLogic.currentProjectId` hadn't loaded yet, causing the API URL to use "@current" instead of the actual team ID (997).

The `currentProjectId` selector in `teamLogic` falls back to "@current" when `currentTeam` hasn't been loaded yet, creating a race condition between:
1. `teamLogic` loading the currentTeam from the app context
2. `selectExistingFeatureFlagModalLogic` building the API URL

## Changes

Fixes race condition in SelectExistingFeatureFlagModal test where API URL becomes undefined/invalid due to timing issues.

- Added `waitFor` to ensure `teamLogic.values.currentProjectId` equals `MOCK_TEAM_ID` before opening the modal
- Added import for `teamLogic` to access currentProjectId directly
- This prevents the race condition and ensures proper test setup ordering

Tested manually in local dev with Elixir GenServer crash events that produce long exception messages. ## Publish to changelog? No

title: feat(llma): enable clustering read tools and add exploring-llm-clusters skill
body:
## Problem

MCP users have no way to explore LLM analytics clusters. The clustering infrastructure is mature (models, API, frontend), but all MCP tools are disabled and there's no guidance for agents on how to query cluster results.

## Changes

- **Enable 2 read-only MCP tools** in `products/llm_analytics/mcp/tools.yaml`:
  - `llm-analytics-clustering-jobs-list` — list clustering job configs, with a hint that cluster results are queryable via `docs-search` or `execute-sql` on `$ai_trace_clusters`/`$ai_generation_clusters` events
  - `llm-analytics-clustering-jobs-retrieve` — get a specific job by ID
- **Add `exploring-llm-clusters` skill** (`products/llm_analytics/skills/exploring-llm-clusters/`):
  - `SKILL.md` — cluster event schema, SQL query patterns, investigation workflows, UI link construction
  - `scripts/print_clusters.py` — parses cluster result JSON into a readable summary with run metadata (job name, level, window, run ID)
- **Infra**: exclude skill scripts from ruff (`pyproject.toml`) and lint-staged (`package.json`)

## How did you test this code?

Agent-authored PR. Verified:
- ruff exclusion works (`ruff check products/llm_analytics/skills/` passes)
- lint-staged glob correctly excludes skill scripts (commit hook passes)
- markdownlint passes on SKILL.md
- Tool descriptions match existing patterns from `prompts.yaml`

No backend logic changes — only YAML config and new skill files.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

No docs changes needed — cluster event schema is already documented in the trace clustering README.

## 🤖 LLM context

Agent-authored. Key design decisions made during the session:
- Enabled only read tools (jobs list/retrieve), not write/trigger tools
- Dropped `clustering-config-retrieve` — legacy singleton superseded by jobs
- Added hint in `clustering-jobs-list` description pointing agents to `docs-search`/`execute-sql` for cluster results, so the discovery path works without the skill loaded
- Kept only `print_clusters.py` script — avoided opinionated scripts (compare, extract) in favor of letting agents decide their own exploration path
- `llma-mcp` prompt (PostHog prompt store, v8) updated to reflect new tools

title: feat: Add Settings section to context panel

labels.name: update-snapshots
labels.description: Enable auto commit snapshots for Storybook and Playwright

body:
## Problem

The current settings architecture has product-specific settings scattered across different locations, making it difficult for users to find and configure settings for various PostHog products. This creates a fragmented user experience where settings are buried in product-specific tabs or separate scenes.

## Changes

This PR consolidates product settings into a unified settings panel accessible from the side navigation. Key changes include:

**Settings Architecture:**
- Refactored the SupportedPlatforms component to use a more flexible layout with proper wrapping and improved styling
- Added `settingsSection` property to scene configurations to link products to their settings sections
- Removed dedicated settings tabs/scenes from individual products (Experiments, Surveys, Visual Review, etc.)
- Consolidated conversations settings into reusable components under a new settings section

**Product-Specific Changes:**
- **Conversations**: Moved all settings from `/support/settings` to individual setting components, added redirect to new settings location
- **Visual Review**: Moved repository configuration from dedicated scene to settings panel
- **Experiments**: Removed settings tab, moved configuration to main settings
- **Surveys**: Removed settings tab, updated banner to open settings panel
- **Logs**: Removed configuration tab, streamlined to viewer-only interface

**Navigation Updates:**
- Updated product routes to remove settings-specific paths
- Added redirects from old settings URLs to new unified settings locations
- Updated scene configurations to include `settingsSection` mappings for context-aware settings

**Component Improvements:**
- Enhanced SupportedPlatforms component with better responsive design and icon sizing
- Refactored settings components to be more modular and reusable
- Improved settings panel integration with proper section linking

## How did you test this code?

As an AI agent, I have not manually tested this code. The changes involve:

- Structural refactoring of settings components and routing
- Moving settings from product-specific locations to unified settings panel
- Updating navigation and URL handling for settings access

Manual testing should verify:
1. All product settings are accessible through the unified settings panel
2. Redirects from old settings URLs work correctly
3. Settings functionality remains intact after component moves
4. Context-aware settings panel opens to correct sections from product pages
5. SupportedPlatforms component displays correctly across different screen sizes

## Publish to changelog?

No - this is primarily an internal refactoring to improve settings organization and user experience.

## Docs update

Settings documentation may need updates to reflect the new unified settings location and removal of product-specific settings pages.

The side panel system was overly complex with multiple deprecated features and inconsistent navigation patterns. The docs and changelog panels used external iframes that were unreliable in testing environments, and the status/health panels had overlapping functionality. The navigation logic was convoluted with special cases for different tab visibility states.

## Changes

- **Removed deprecated panels**: Docs, Changelog, Status, and Health panels that were either unused or had better alternatives
- **Simplified navigation**: Removed complex visibility logic and "extra tabs" concept - tabs are now either visible in nav or openable programmatically
- **Consolidated health logic**: Moved pipeline health logic to its dedicated scene location

- **Streamlined settings**: Settings panel now uses scene context for default section instead of complex persistence logic
- **Removed external dependencies**: Eliminated iframe-based panels that caused testing issues

The side panel now focuses on core functionality: Max AI, Notebooks, Activity, Discussion, Access Control, Info, Settings (contextual), Exports, and Support.

title: feat(cohorts): allow creating static cohorts from criteria
body: Issue https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/issues/47701 ## Problem Static cohorts could not be created directly from a set of criteria. Users had to create a dynamic cohort first, duplicate it as static, and then delete the dynamic cohort. That workflow was unintuitive for common use cases like survey targeting, where users expect to snapshot a set of matching people in one step. This PR adds first-class support for creating static cohorts from criteria, while keeping the existing CSV upload and manual-add flows intact. It also improves the editor UX by clearly showing when a static cohort was snapshotted from criteria and making those criteria read-only after creation, rather than leaving users to guess what is or is not supported. This PR does not address the separate cohort deletion refetch issue. ## Changes - Added a new `Populate from` flow for static cohorts in the cohort editor: - `Upload or add people` - `Criteria` - Kept `Upload or add people` as the default option for new static cohorts. - Reused the existing cohort criteria builder for `Static + Criteria`. - Added explanatory UI copy for static cohorts created from criteria, including a read-only state for existing static cohorts created this way. - Fixed the layout issue in the read-only static-criteria section that caused a large blank white area above the criteria block. - Updated frontend cohort form logic so static cohorts created from criteria validate and submit real `filters`, while the legacy static upload/manual path continues to submit empty filters. - Added backend support for creating static cohorts from saved criteria filters. - Added a one-time background task to snapshot matching people into static cohort membership from filters. - Prevented editing criteria on existing static cohorts created from criteria in this first iteration. - Extended stuck static cohort recovery to support criteria-based static cohorts in addition to query-based static cohorts. ## How did you test this code? Automated tests only. Ran targeted frontend tests: ``` frontend/src/scenes/cohorts/cohortEditLogic.test.ts frontend/src/scenes/cohorts/cohortsSceneLogic.test.ts frontend/src/models/cohortsModel.test.ts ``` Ran targeted backend API tests: ``` posthog/api/test/test_cohort.py -k 'static_cohort_create_with_criteria or static_cohort_rejects_criteria_edits_after_creation'" ``` Ran backend cohort task tests: ``` posthog/tasks/test/test_calculate_cohort.py" ``` Expected behavior verified by code and test coverage: - A static cohort can now be created directly from criteria. - Existing static CSV/manual creation flows still work. - Existing static cohorts created from criteria show criteria in a disabled/read-only state. - Criteria edits on existing static cohorts created from criteria are rejected by the backend. ## Publish to changelog? no ## Docs update No docs update required.

title: refactor(ingestion): static output and producer config with compile-time key validation
body:
## Problem

The ingestion outputs config (topics, producers) was not part of the server config object system. Output definitions read `process.env` directly, making it hard to discover all server configuration and impossible for the type system to enforce that required config keys exist.

## Changes

Introduce builder patterns for both the Kafka producer registry and ingestion outputs that enforce config keys at compile time:

**`KafkaProducerRegistryBuilder`** — replaces direct `KafkaProducerRegistry` construction. Each `.register(name, configMap, config)` call validates that the config object contains all env var keys from the mapping. Producers are created eagerly at `.build()` time.

**`IngestionOutputsBuilder`** — replaces `resolveIngestionOutputs()`. Each `.register(name, { topicKey, producerKey })` call accumulates config key requirements in the type parameters. `.build(registry, config)` then checks all keys exist in the config and that producer values match the registry's producer name type.

**Config types** — `KafkaProducerEnvConfig`, `IngestionOutputsConfig`, `ErrorTrackingOutputsConfig` added to server config types, with defaults in `getDefault*Config()` functions. Topic and producer defaults now live in the config object (via `overrideWithEnv`) instead of in output definitions.

**`KafkaProducerRegistry`** simplified — no longer creates producers lazily. Accepts `Record<P, KafkaProducerWrapper>` from the builder, provides typed `getProducer(name: P)` access.

## How did you test this code?

Unit tests updated for `KafkaProducerRegistry` and `resolveIngestionOutputs`. All pass.

Compile-time validation verified by introducing deliberate errors and confirming `pnpm build` rejects them:

| Scenario | Change | Error location |
|---|---|---|
| Typo in output config key | `topicKey: 'INGESTION_OUTPUT_EVENTS_TOPICC'` | `.build(registry, config)` — config missing typo'd key |
| Missing output config from server type | Comment out `IngestionOutputsConfig &` | `.build(registry, config)` — config missing all output keys |
| Producer value typed as `string` instead of `ProducerName` | Change type in `IngestionOutputsConfig` | `.build(registry, config)` — `string` too wide for `Record<PK, P>` |
| Missing default in `getDefaultIngestionOutputsConfig()` | Delete one entry | Return type — property missing from `IngestionOutputsConfig` |
| Missing producer env config from server type | Comment out `KafkaProducerEnvConfig &` | `.register()` on producer builder — config missing producer env var keys |

## Publish to changelog?

no

title: fix(cohorts): treat missing precalculated-person-properties as matches=false
body:
## Problem

Real-time cohort calculations were treating missing entries in `precalculated_person_properties` as "unknown" rather than "false", causing incorrect cohort membership results.

The issue was in the HogQL queries that use `argMax(matches, _timestamp)` which returns `null` for missing entries. When `countIf` encounters null values, it ignores them rather than treating them as false, leading to incorrect cohort calculations.

## Changes

- Modified `HogQLRealtimeCohortQuery` to use `ifNull(argMax(matches, _timestamp), 0)` instead of `argMax(matches, _timestamp)` in all person property queries
- Updated three key query methods:
  - `get_person_condition()`: Single condition queries
  - `_build_or_semantics_query()`: OR logic for merged properties  
  - `_build_and_semantics_query()`: AND logic for merged properties
- Added comprehensive tests for missing property handling scenarios

## How did you test this code?

- Added three new test cases covering single condition, OR semantics, and AND semantics scenarios
- Verified all existing tests still pass to ensure no regression
- Tests confirm that missing properties are now correctly treated as `matches=false`
- Ran full test suite for `posthog/hogql_queries/test/test_hogql_cohort_query.py` - all 26 tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Release notes

Sourced from urllib3's releases.

2.6.3

🚀 urllib3 is fundraising for HTTP/2 support

urllib3 is raising ~$40,000 USD to release HTTP/2 support and ensure long-term sustainable maintenance of the project after a sharp decline in financial support. If your company or organization uses Python and would benefit from HTTP/2 support in Requests, pip, cloud SDKs, and thousands of other projects please consider contributing financially to ensure HTTP/2 support is developed sustainably and maintained for the long-haul.

Thank you for your support.

Changes

  • Fixed a security issue where decompression-bomb safeguards of the streaming API were bypassed when HTTP redirects were followed. (CVE-2026-21441 reported by @​D47A, 8.9 High, GHSA-38jv-5279-wg99)
  • Started treating Retry-After times greater than 6 hours as 6 hours by default. (urllib3/urllib3#3743)
  • Fixed urllib3.connection.VerifiedHTTPSConnection on Emscripten. (urllib3/urllib3#3752)

title: fix(ci): temporal client thread cleanup on command exit
body:
## Problem

Jovan: CI sporadically fails with:
```
    ] Registering 3 search attribute(s) [posthog.management.commands.register_temporal_search_attributes] attributes=['PostHogTeamId', 'PostHogOrgId', 'PostHogDagId'] pid=9751 tid=140608323758976
    ] Done                           [posthog.management.commands.register_temporal_search_attributes] pid=9751 tid=140608323758976
Fatal Python error: PyGILState_Release: thread state 0x7fe19c00f8f0 must be current when releasing
Python runtime state: finalizing (tstate=0x00007fe1ed28ba30)

Thread 0x00007fe1ed30ab80 (most recent call first):
  <no Python frame>
```
Claude:
The Temporal client's Rust/gRPC bridge spawns background threads that can outlive the event loop. During CPython finalization, these threads can cause a crash with "PyGILState_Release: thread state … must be current" followed by SIGABRT when the GIL is destroyed while threads are still active.

## Changes

Added `os._exit(0)` call at the end of the `handle()` method in the `register_temporal_search_attributes` management command to skip interpreter teardown entirely once the command completes. This prevents the Python interpreter from attempting to clean up Temporal's background threads during finalization.

Also added the necessary `import os` statement.

## How did you test this code?

This is a targeted fix for a known issue with Temporal client cleanup. The change is minimal and only affects the exit behavior of this specific management command after it has completed its work. Existing tests should continue to pass, and the command will now exit cleanly without triggering GIL-related crashes during finalization.

## Docs update

No documentation update needed.

title: fix(pinterest_ads): handle missing stats columns by returning zero values
body:
## Problem

Pinterest Ads adapter crashes with "Field not found: total_clickthrough" when querying the marketing analytics overview. This happens when the data warehouse table doesn't have certain columns in its metadata (e.g., the sync didn't include clickthrough data yet, or the column is marked as invalid).

The adapter already had defensive `_has_stats_column()` checks for optional fields (`total_conversions`, `total_checkout_value_in_micro_dollar`) but was missing them for core fields (`total_impression`, `total_clickthrough`, `spend_in_dollar`).

## Changes

- Add `_has_stats_column()` guards to `_get_impressions_field()`, `_get_clicks_field()`, and `_get_cost_field()` in the Pinterest Ads adapter, returning `toFloat(0)` when the column is missing — same pattern already used for conversion fields
- Update test mock to include column metadata so the snapshot test continues to verify the full query generation path

## How did you test this code?

- Existing `test_pinterest_ads_adapter_with_real_data` test validates the full query with real CSV data (columns present)
- Updated `test_pinterest_ads_native_query_generation` mock to include column metadata, ensuring snapshot stays valid
- Manually tested against a live Pinterest Ads account

## Publish to changelog?

No

title: feat(kafka-assigner): integrate K8s awareness for smarter rebalancing
body:
## Problem

The kafka-assigner's Deregister RPC currently returns `ShutdownNow` for all consumers, regardless of whether the pod is being rolled out, downscaled, or crashing. This means partitions are never gracefully drained during Deployment rollouts or downscales, causing brief processing gaps while the coordinator waits for lease expiry to reassign orphaned partitions.

During rebalancing, the coordinator also has no visibility into K8s controller intent, so it may assign partitions to pods that are about to be terminated (old-generation pods in a rolling update) or unnecessarily move partitions away from pods that will return under the same name (StatefulSet rollouts).

## Changes

- Integrate the `k8s-awareness` crate into the kafka-assigner to classify pod departures and adjust rebalancing decisions based on K8s controller intent
- On `Register`, discover the consumer's K8s controller (Deployment vs StatefulSet) and generation hash, storing them in the `RegisteredConsumer` record in etcd
- On `Deregister`, classify the departure reason: StatefulSet rollouts return `ShutdownNow` (pod name returns), while Deployment rollouts and downscales return `WaitForDrain` so partitions are handed off before shutdown
- Add `filter_consumers_for_k8s()` to the coordinator's rebalance loop, which excludes old-generation Ready consumers during Deployment rollouts and re-includes Draining consumers during StatefulSet rollouts
- Add `k8s_awareness_enabled` and `k8s_namespace` config fields, disabled by default for non-K8s environments
- Add k3s testcontainers integration tests that validate departure classification in a real K8s environment

## How did you test this code?

title: feat(web-analytics): Add notable changes query runner
body:
## Problem
There's currently no easy way to find the most notable changes across Web Analytics data. 

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->
<!-- Closes #ISSUE_ID -->

## Changes
Adds a new Notable Changes query runner for web analytics. This surfaces the most significant traffic changes across multiple dimensions, comparing the current period to the previous period.

The scoring system works in two stages:
Filtering: A dimension value must have at least 10 combined visitors (current + previous) to be considered. This eliminates noise from low-traffic pages/sources.

Scoring:
impact_score = abs(percent_change) * sqrt(baseline).  This means a 100% increase on a high-traffic page ranks above a 100% increase on a low-traffic page.
Percent change: (current - previous) / previous. For brand-new items (previous=0), capped at min(10.0, current).
Baseline: max(current, previous)

## Problem

The signal report summary prompt had sections ordered as: why it matters → what's happening → the bottom line. Starting with impact before facts felt backwards — readers want to know _what_ before _why_.

## Changes

Reorders the Axios-style brief sections to: tl;dr → what's happening → why it matters → the bottom line. This gives the reader a quick summary upfront, then concrete facts, then impact, then next steps.

The MCP `query-traces-list` tool had an incomplete prompt, missing response fields, and several AI properties were absent from the taxonomy. This made the tool less useful for LLM consumers trying to understand trace data.

## Changes

- Renamed MCP tool from `query-traces-list` to `query-llm-traces-list` for consistency

title: feat: add unified hogli build command with "smart" change detection
body:
## Problem

When developing locally, developers need to remember whether to run `hogli build:schema` or `hogli build:openapi` (or both) depending on what they changed.

## Changes

Adds a unified `hogli build` command with smart change detection as the default.

- `hogli build` -- detects changed files via git diff against merge-base with master + uncommitted/staged/untracked, matches against per-command trigger globs, runs only what's needed
- `hogli build --force` -- rebuilds all pipelines unconditionally
- `hogli build --dry-run` -- shows what would rebuild without executing
- `hogli build --list` -- lists all registered pipelines and their trigger globs

Covers 7 codegen pipelines: schema, openapi, grammar, taxonomy, products, skills, schema-mcp. Pipelines continue on failure so independent work isn't blocked.

Architecture is intentionally simple: a dict mapping command names to trigger globs, a git change detector, and a sequential loop. No dataclasses, no parallel execution machinery.

New files:
- `common/hogli/build.py` -- trigger registry, git-based change detection, Click command
- `common/hogli/tests/test_build.py` -- 34 tests covering matching, detection, CLI

## How did you test this code?

- 34 unit tests passing (`pytest common/hogli/tests/test_build.py`)
- Verified `hogli build --list`, `hogli build --dry-run`, `hogli build --force --dry-run` produce correct output
- Verified command appears in `hogli --help` under "Build and generate code" category

## Publish to changelog?

No

## LLM context

Co-authored with Claude Code. Simplified from an earlier version that had a `Pipeline` dataclass and parallel execution via `ThreadPoolExecutor` -- removed after review showed the complexity wasn't justified.

title: feat(hogli): add doctor health check and post-command hints
body:
## Problem

Developers forget to run maintenance commands like `hogli doctor:disk` and `hogli doctor:zombies`, or sometimes don't even know these commands exists. 

There's also no quick way to get an overview of dev environment health without running each command individually.

## Changes

Two new features for hogli:

**1. `hogli doctor` -- unified health check**

Runs four fast, read-only probes and prints a status summary:

```
hogli doctor

  Disk usage ............... OK (22.6 KiB reclaimable)
  Zombie processes ......... OK (0 orphaned)
  Docker ................... OK (daemon running)
  Migrations ............... WARNING (1 unapplied)
                              run `hogli migrations:sync`

  1 warning(s) found.
```

Reuses existing `_estimate_*` functions and `_scan_posthog_processes` -- no new heavy logic.

**2. Post-command contextual hints**

After any hogli command, occasionally shows a one-liner nudge (npm audit style):

```
  Hint: Run `hogli doctor:disk` to check for disk bloat
```

- Rate-limited to 1 hint per 24 hours
- Suppressed by `HOGLI_NO_HINTS=1`, in CI, and for doctor/telemetry/meta commands
- State persisted at `~/.config/posthog/hogli_hints.json`
- Prints to stderr in dim color

**Files changed:**
- `common/hogli/hints.py` -- new module for hint state and evaluation
- `common/hogli/doctor.py` -- new `hogli doctor` command + `record_check_run` calls in existing commands
- `common/hogli/core/cli.py` -- wire `maybe_show_hint` into `CategorizedGroup.invoke()`
- `common/hogli/tests/test_hints.py` -- 18 tests

## How did you test this code?

- 220 hogli tests pass (`pytest common/hogli/tests/`)
- Smoke tested `hogli doctor` locally
- Verified hint state file is created and updated
- Verified suppression via `HOGLI_NO_HINTS=1`
- Verified hints are skipped for doctor/meta/telemetry commands

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

N/A

## LLM context

Co-authored with Claude Code (Opus 4.6)

title: fix(phai): add query type reference to read_taxonomy and wrap validation errors
body:
## Problem

LLMs calling the `read_taxonomy` tool produce ~22 preventable validation errors per month (out of ~154K calls). Analysis of traces from the last 30 days shows three categories:

1. **Wrong field names (13 hits):** LLM sends `event` instead of `event_name`
2. **Wrong discriminator values (8 hits):** LLM sends class names like `ReadEvents` instead of `events`, or invented kinds like `person_properties`
3. **Other wrong fields (1 hit):** Missing `property_name`

These errors are recoverable (the LLM retries successfully), but waste tokens and add latency.

## Changes

- Added a "Query types" reference section to the tool description listing exact `kind` values and required field names, giving the LLM an unambiguous quick reference
- Wrapped `ValidationError` in `MaxToolRetryableError` in `_run_impl` so any remaining validation errors follow the established retry error pattern instead of propagating as raw exceptions

## How did you test this code?

- Added a test confirming `_run_impl` raises `MaxToolRetryableError` (not raw `ValidationError`) when given invalid input (e.g., `event` instead of `event_name`)
- All existing tests pass: `pytest ee/hogai/tools/read_taxonomy/test/ -x` (10 passed)

I'm an agent — I have not tested this manually beyond the automated test suite.

## Publish to changelog?

no

## 🤖 LLM context

Investigation started from PostHog trace `cd342414-5f98-4240-81b7-b0b553384413` which showed a `ValidationError` on the `read_taxonomy` tool. Used PostHog MCP SQL queries to analyze error frequency and categories across the last 30 days before designing the fix.

title: feat(visual-review): add SSIM fallback for tall-page dilution

labels.name: waiting
labels.description: Prevents stale-bot from marking the PR as stale.

body:
## Problem

Visual review's pixelmatch counts differing pixels as a percentage of total image area. On tall pages, a real UI change at the bottom falls below the 1% threshold because most pixels are identical. This caused VR to miss changes that Storybook (using SSIM) correctly caught.

Discovered on #52937 — light-theme replay settings snapshots had a visible new button but only 0.65% pixel diff (below 1% threshold), while SSIM dissimilarity was 7%.

## Changes

**Two-tier classification** in the Celery diff task:
- Pixelmatch ratio >= 1% → CHANGED (fast path, covers ~95% of cases)
- SSIM dissimilarity >= 1% → CHANGED (safety net for tall-page dilution)
- Both below → UNCHANGED (genuine noise)

**New ssim.py** — pure numpy SSIM (Wang et al., 2004) with 11x11 windows using cumulative-sum box filter. Zero new dependencies, validated within 0.07% of scikit-image.

SSIM adds ~330ms but only runs on borderline cases where pixelmatch is below threshold.

Designed for easy swap to a Rust implementation ([pixelhog](https://github.com/PostHog/pixelhog)) once published — only diff.py and ssim.py would change, task logic and tests stay the same.

## How did you test this code?

Agent-authored. Ran test_diff.py (6/6 pass) and new test_two_tier_diff.py (9/9 pass). The test suite includes a synthetic tall-page fixture (400x3000) that reproduces the exact dilution scenario: pixelmatch sees 0.65% diff (below threshold), SSIM sees 1.36% dissimilarity (above threshold), classifier correctly returns changed_by_ssim.

Validated SSIM against scikit-image on real snapshot pairs from #52937. No manual testing.

## 🤖 LLM context

Investigated VR vs Storybook disagreement on #52937. Built and benchmarked a Rust replacement (pixelhog) — 80x faster pixelmatch, 2.4x faster SSIM — but deferred the dependency swap until it's published on PyPI. This PR ships the classification improvement with zero new deps.
