title: feat(sig): restructure pause API as signal_processing resource
body:
## Problem

The grouping v2 pipeline needs the ability to be paused temporarily. The previous iteration had a standalone viewset; this restructures it as a proper REST resource at `signal_processing/`.

## Changes

**Workflow (`grouping_v2.py`)**:
- Added `set_paused_until` signal, `clear_paused` signal, and `get_paused_state` query
- Pause loop: when paused, sleeps 30s intervals via `wait_condition`, `continue_as_new` after 30 min
- Three classmethods (`pause_until`, `unpause`, `paused_state`) using signal-with-start pattern
- `paused_until` carried across `continue_as_new` via `TeamSignalGroupingV2Input`

**Views (`views.py`)**:
- `SignalProcessingViewSet` with:
  - `GET /signal_processing/` — returns current pause state
  - `PUT /signal_processing/pause/` — sets pause timestamp
  - `DELETE /signal_processing/pause/` — clears pause
- Explicit request/response serializers for accurate OpenAPI schema

**Types (`types.py`)**:
- Added `paused_until: Optional[datetime]` to `TeamSignalGroupingV2Input`

## How did you test this code?

This is an agent-authored PR. No manual testing was performed. The code compiles and follows the same signal-with-start pattern used by the buffer workflow.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

N/A

## 🤖 LLM context

Agent-authored PR. Restructured from a previous iteration that had separate `/pause`, `/unpause`, `/state` endpoints into a single `signal_processing` resource with RESTful verbs.

title: feat(hogql): add aliases for ClickHouse type conversion functions
body:
## Problem

Users writing SQL queries in the PostHog SQL editor frequently hit parsing errors when using standard ClickHouse type conversion function names like `toFloat64`, `toFloat64OrNull`, `toInt64`, `toInt32`, etc. HogQL only exposed simplified versions (`toFloat`, `toInt`), causing queries to fail with unhelpful errors. This is especially common with AI-generated SQL which produces standard ClickHouse function names.

Multiple user reports across two weeks confirm this as a recurring blocker for marketing dashboards and funnel analyses involving type conversions for metrics like CAC and conversion rates. A new error tracking issue (`QueryError: Unsupported type cast to 'float64'`) was also created from production.

## Changes

- **`posthog/hogql/functions/clickhouse/conversions.py`**: Added public aliases for common ClickHouse type conversion functions that map to safe `accurateCastOrNull` calls:
  - Integer: `toInt8`, `toInt16`, `toInt32`, `toInt64`, `toInt128`, `toInt256`
  - Unsigned integer: `toUInt8` through `toUInt256`
  - Float: `toFloat32`, `toFloat64`
  - OrNull variants: `toFloat64OrNull`, `toFloat32OrNull`, `toFloatOrNull`, `toInt8OrNull` through `toInt64OrNull`
  - OrZero variant: `toFloat64OrZero`

- **`posthog/hogql/printer/base.py`**: Extended `visit_type_cast` to accept ClickHouse-style type names in `::` cast syntax (`float32`, `float64`, `int8`–`int256`, `uint8`–`uint256`, `bigint`)

- **`posthog/hogql/printer/postgres_functions.py`**: Added Postgres dialect mappings for all new function aliases

- **`posthog/hogql/printer/test/test_printer.py`**: Added test cases for new function aliases and type cast names

## How did you test this code?

Added parameterized test cases for the new type cast names and inline assertions for the new function aliases. This is an agent-authored PR — no manual testing was performed.

## Publish to changelog?

no

## Docs update

The [HogQL functions documentation](https://posthog.com/docs/sql/clickhouse-functions) should be updated to list these as supported aliases.

## 🤖 LLM context

Agent-authored PR addressing signal analysis showing recurring user failures with ClickHouse type conversion functions in the SQL editor. The fix adds safe aliases (all routing through `accurateCastOrNull`) rather than exposing raw ClickHouse conversion functions, maintaining the existing safety guarantees.

title: fix(billing): prevent API errors from crashing SidePanel ErrorBoundary
body:
## Problem

When the billing service returns non-OK responses, kea-loaders in `billingSpendLogic`, `billingUsageLogic`, and `billingLogic` re-throw the API error after showing a toast. This propagates through kea-loaders into React's render cycle and crashes the SidePanel ErrorBoundary with "Non-OK response". Additionally, most billing service proxy calls in `billing_manager.py` lack HTTP timeouts, contributing to UI lag when the billing service is slow or unresponsive.

## Changes

**Frontend (error handling):**
- `billingSpendLogic.ts`: Return `null` instead of re-throwing in `loadBillingSpend` catch block
- `billingUsageLogic.ts`: Return `null` instead of re-throwing in `loadBillingUsage` catch block
- `billingLogic.tsx`: Wrap `loadBilling` in try/catch returning `null` on failure; return `null` instead of re-throwing in `updateBillingLimits`

**Backend (HTTP timeouts):**
- `billing_manager.py`: Add `timeout=20` to all `requests.get/post/patch` calls that were missing it (17 call sites). Two calls (`deauthorize`, `handle_billing_provider_webhook`) already had `timeout=30` and were left unchanged.

## How did you test this code?

This is an agent-authored PR. The changes are mechanical — replacing `throw error` with `return null` in catch blocks and adding `timeout=20` keyword arguments. No manual testing was performed.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

No docs changes needed.

## 🤖 LLM context

Agent-authored fix based on error tracking signals showing "Non-OK response" errors crashing the SidePanel ErrorBoundary when billing API calls fail.

title: feat(hogql): support table column aliases across all dialects
body:
## Problem

HogQL blocked table column aliases (e.g. `FROM events AS e(a, b, c)`) in non-postgres dialects with "table column aliases are not allowed in {dialect}". This prevented using a standard SQL feature for renaming columns at the table binding level.

## Changes

Introduces `ColumnAliasedTableType` — a new AST type that changes a table binding's visible schema when column aliases are provided. This properly implements SQL table column alias semantics:

- `e.a` resolves correctly through the aliased binding (maps to `uuid` internally)
- `e.uuid` fails — original names are hidden when aliased
- `SELECT *` expands to aliased names
- `WHERE e.c IS NOT NULL` works
- Fewer aliases than columns: remaining columns keep their original names

**Dialect-specific printing:**
- **Postgres:** uses aliased names (`e.a`) since postgres handles `(a, b, c)` natively
- **ClickHouse/HogQL:** uses real DB column names (`e.uuid`) since these dialects don't support the syntax
- **Subqueries:** column aliases are baked into the inner SELECT as `AS` aliases for non-postgres

**Additional fixes:**
- Alias-name collision: `FROM events AS e(event)` no longer corrupts the mapping when an alias name matches an existing column
- Silent alias drop: `FROM events(a, b, c)` without explicit `AS` now works
- `ExpressionField`s accessed through column aliases get `isolate_scope=True` to avoid broken references
- Added `ColumnAliasedTableType` to all `isinstance(x, TableAliasType)` checks across the codebase (resolver, resolver_utils, lazy_tables, clickhouse printer, timestamp_visitor, where_clause_extractor)

## How did you test this code?

- 20 new printer tests across ClickHouse and postgres dialects covering: explicit refs, WHERE clauses, unqualified refs, `SELECT *`, original name rejection, subquery alias baking, arg validation errors
- 6 new resolver tests covering: type creation, remaining column behavior, original name rejection, star expansion, unqualified resolution, excess aliases
- Manually verified queries like `SELECT e.a FROM events AS e(a, b, c) WHERE e.c IS NOT NULL`

## Publish to changelog?

no

## 🤖 LLM context

Agent-authored PR. Co-authored with Claude Code (Opus 4.6).

title: feat(ws): persistent Redis-backed retry tracking for pipeline v3 load consumer
body:
## Problem

warehouse-source-load consumer tracks retries in-process memory, which means OOM crashes or process restarts lose all retry state. A message that repeatedly causes OOM can be retried indefinitely, never reaching the DLQ, and the job stays stuck in a running state forever.

## Changes

  - Added retry_tracker.py — Redis-backed persistent retry tracking with RetryInfo dataclass, error classification, and configurable limits per error type
  - Changed _process_batch_with_retry from in-memory batch-level retry loop to per-message persistent retry with pre-increment
  - Added _process_single_with_inprocess_retry for fast in-process transient error recovery
  - Moved _mark_job_failed call from processor.py to consumer level
  - Added _extract_message_key helper for extracting retry tracking key fields
  - Added runbook for replaying load messages from S3 parquet files

## How did you test this code?

Local run
Test pass

## Publish to changelog?

NO

Currently it infers the region from the ticket session context and redirects to either EU/US admin using the user's email as a query string. In the next PR in the stack we'll login automatically from the `ImpersonationNotice` without a round trip to Django admin. ## How did you test this code? title: fix(devex): inline trivial celery tasks that don't need async dispatch
body:
## Problem

`identify_task` and `calculate_product_activation` were registered as Celery tasks dispatched on every user/team serialization. The actual work is trivial — a single `posthoganalytics.capture()` call (already non-blocking via SDK background thread) and a DB check that almost always bails out early. The Celery overhead (broker round-trip, worker scheduling, deserialization) far exceeds the work itself.

## Changes

- Inline `posthoganalytics.capture` directly in `UserSerializer.to_representation` and delete the `user_identify` task module
- Remove `@shared_task` from `calculate_product_activation` and call it as a plain function in the team/project serializers
- Update test mocks and task registration snapshot

## How did you test this code?

Ran the full affected test suites (test_user, test_team, test_authentication, test_signup, test_product_intent, test_task_registration) — all passing.

## Publish to changelog?

no

## 🤖 LLM context

Agent-authored PR. No manual testing beyond the automated test suites listed above.

title: fix(signup): skip redundant Radar API call on challenge resubmit
body:
## Problem

When a user completes a Turnstile challenge and resubmits, `evaluate_auth_attempt` calls the Radar API again, which returns CHALLENGE a second time. This causes `would_challenge` to be logged twice per successful challenge flow, making the "Would be challenged" metric consistently 2x the actual challenge count.

## Changes

- Extract `_evaluate_verdict()` — skips the Radar API call when `turnstile_token` + `challenge_nonce` are present (resubmit), returns `CHALLENGE` verdict directly
- Extract `_decide_outcome()` — flat guard-clause chain replacing nested if/else blocks, returns a string outcome (`allow`, `block`, `bypass`, `challenge`, `completed`)
- `evaluate_auth_attempt` now orchestrates: get verdict → decide outcome → log → act
- Add `mock_call_api.assert_not_called()` to 4 existing challenge resubmit tests

## How did you test this code?

- 41 existing unit tests pass, 4 tightened with `assert_not_called` on the Radar API mock

## Publish to changelog?

no

title: fix(sql-editor): back/forward with insights
body:
## Problem

https://posthog.slack.com/archives/C0ACRAMJUAG/p1774533598372539

Root cause was in the SQL editor URL rehydration  logic. The editor only reopened `#insight=...`,  `#view=...`, and `#draft=...` targets on first mount,  because those branches were gated on  `values.queryInput === null`. After switching to a  plain SQL query in the same mounted editor, browser  back/forward could restore a URL that still  contained `#insight=...` while the in-memory tab stayed as an unsaved query, which is why users saw "Save as insight" instead of "Update insight"

## Changes

Fix this. Compare the object the URL is pointing us towards at all point, not just on first mount.

## How did you test this code?

Added tests

## Publish to changelog?

no, just a bugfix

## Docs update

no, just a bugfix

title: feat(data-warehouse): add cancel sync button for schema syncs

labels.name: update-snapshots
labels.description: Enable auto commit snapshots for Storybook and Playwright

body:
## Problem

Users cannot cancel a running data warehouse sync. The only way to stop a sync is to trigger a resync (which cancels then restarts) or wait for it to finish.

## Changes

Adds a "Cancel sync" option to the schema actions menu (the `...` / `More` dropdown), visible when a sync is running. The backend cancels the Temporal workflow via the existing `cancel_external_data_workflow` helper. The workflow's error handling then sets the schema status to `Failed` with `latest_error = "Cancelled"`.

- **Backend:** new `cancel` action on `ExternalDataSchemaViewSet`
- **Frontend:** `cancel()` API method, `cancelSchema` kea action/listener, "Cancel sync" button in the `More` menu

## How did you test this code?

This PR was authored by an AI agent. No manual testing was performed. The `cancel` action follows the same pattern already used inside the `resync` endpoint.

## Publish to changelog?

no

## Docs update

N/A

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored by Claude Code. The cancel action reuses the existing `cancel_external_data_workflow` infrastructure that `resync` already calls internally. The Temporal workflow's `except Exception` handler catches the `CancelledError` and sets the job/schema to `Failed` with error "Cancelled".

title: feat(llma): use PostHog SDK integration for stamphog LLM analytics
body:
## Problem

The stamphog PR approval agent has no observability into LLM performance, costs, and behavior. PR #52984 added a hand-rolled 202-line analytics module, but PostHog's Python SDK now has a proper Claude Agent SDK integration (`posthog.ai.claude_agent_sdk`) that handles this automatically.

## Changes

Minimal change (21 lines added) to wire stamphog into the SDK integration:

- **`reviewer.py`**: Import `query` from `posthoganalytics.ai.claude_agent_sdk` instead of `claude_agent_sdk`. Falls back to the original if posthoganalytics is not installed. Passes PR metadata (number, repo, author, tier, gate verdict) as custom properties.
- **`review_pr.py`**: Add `posthoganalytics` to PEP 723 script dependencies.

The SDK integration automatically emits `$ai_generation` (per LLM turn), `$ai_span` (per tool use), and `$ai_trace` (per query) events with token counts, costs, latency, input/output, and cache metrics.

Depends on PostHog/posthog-python#477.

## How did you test this code?

This is an agent-authored change. The PostHog SDK integration was tested separately:
- 16 unit tests passing in posthog-python
- Live tested against EU PostHog project with multi-turn tool-calling queries
- Verified generation, span, and trace events appear correctly in LLM Analytics dashboard

No changes to stamphog's review behavior — the `query()` wrapper is a transparent pass-through.

## Publish to changelog?

No

title: feat(logs+replay): Add logs support to session replay inspector

labels.name: update-snapshots
labels.description: Enable auto commit snapshots for Storybook and Playwright

body:
## Problem

Session replay users need visibility into logs that occurred during a recorded session to better understand the full context of user interactions and system behavior. Currently, the inspector only shows frontend events, console logs, and network activity.

## Changes

Added comprehensive logs support to the session replay inspector:

- **New component `ItemLog`**: Displays log entries in both collapsed and expanded views with severity-based styling (trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)
- **Log querying**: Integrated logs API to fetch logs filtered by session ID and recording time range
- **Mini-filters**: Added three new filter categories for logs:
  - `logs-info`: Info, debug, and trace level logs
  - `logs-warn`: Warning level logs  
  - `logs-error`: Error and fatal level logs
- **Inspector controls**: Added "Logs" filter button with:
  - Loading state while fetching logs
  - Upsell prompt if logs aren't set up
  - Direct link to view logs in the Logs product for the session
- **Grouped log display**: Shows when the same log message occurs multiple times with timestamps
- **Log details panel**: Displays:
  - Full log message body
  - Service/instrumentation scope
  - Log attributes in key-value format
  - Link to view logs in the Logs product

The feature is gated behind the `SESSION_REPLAY_BACKEND_LOGS` feature flag and only appears when logs are available for the session.

## How did you test this code?

- Added comprehensive Storybook stories (`ItemLog.stories.tsx`) covering:
  - All severity levels (trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)
  - Various log content scenarios (long messages, attributes, grouped logs)
  - Collapsed and expanded states
  - Edge cases (no attributes, overflowing text)
- Updated `PlayerInspector.stories.tsx` with mock logs data
- Verified integration with existing inspector filtering and mini-filter logic
- Code follows existing patterns for inspector item types and filtering
- Updated unit tests for miniFiltersLogic

## Publish to changelog?

Yes - this adds logs visibility to session replay, a significant feature for debugging.

## LLM context

This PR was authored by Claude Code. The implementation follows existing patterns in the codebase for inspector item types (like `ItemConsoleLog`, `ItemEvent`). Key architectural decisions:
- Uses the existing `logsIngestionLogic` to check if logs are configured
- Queries `/api/projects/:team_id/logs/query/` with session_id filter
- Reuses `PropertyFilterValue` display component for log attributes
- Follows the same collapsed/expanded pattern as other inspector items

title: feat: accept Stripe Shared Payment Tokens in agentic provisioning
body:
## Problem

Users who select the `pay_as_you_go` plan via `stripe projects add posthog/analytics` need to be billed through Stripe's Shared Payment Token (SPT) mechanism.

## Changes

- **Token exchange**: Returns `payment_credentials: "orchestrator"` (tells Stripe to collect payment for paid plans)
- **Resource provisioning**: Accepts `payment_credentials` from request body, stores SPT in cache keyed by org ID
- **Helpers**: `get_shared_payment_token(org_id)` for the billing service to retrieve the stored SPT

## Dependencies

**Depends on**: PostHog/billing#1832 (uses SPT when creating subscriptions)

## How did you test this code

Draft - needs end-to-end testing with Stripe Projects CLI.

Design doc: `~/dev/.claude/docs/stripe/specs/spt_billing_design.md`

title: feat(insights): implement pinnable tooltip for line graphs
body:
## Problem

Recently, we've added the ability to pin tooltips in SQL insights. 
With this PR, I'm adding the same thing for the other line graph insights. 
The next thing will be to show all the series in the tooltip (the limit is 8 now), allowing it to be scrolled.

## Changes

Use click to pin the tooltip.

When multiple lines overlap, it can be tricky to open the modal for the correct data point.
So:
- If the tooltip has a single series → clicking the chart opens the modal for that data point.
- If the tooltip has multiple series → open the modal directly from the specific series within the tooltip.

Updates `undici` from 7.8.0 to 7.24.0
Release notes

Sourced from undici's releases.

v7.24.0

Undici v7.24.0 Security Release Notes

This release addresses multiple security vulnerabilities in Undici.

Upgrade guidance

All users on v7 should upgrade to v7.24.0 or later.

Fixed advisories

  • GHSA-2mjp-6q6p-2qxm / CVE-2026-1525 (Medium)
    Inconsistent interpretation of HTTP requests (request/response smuggling class issue).

  • GHSA-f269-vfmq-vjvj / CVE-2026-1528 (High)
    Malicious WebSocket 64-bit frame length handling could crash the client.

  • GHSA-phc3-fgpg-7m6h / CVE-2026-2581 (Medium)
    Unbounded memory consumption in deduplication interceptor response buffering (DoS risk).

  • GHSA-4992-7rv2-5pvq / CVE-2026-1527 (Medium)
    CRLF injection via the upgrade option.

  • GHSA-v9p9-hfj2-hcw8 / CVE-2026-2229 (High)
    Unhandled exception from invalid server_max_window_bits in WebSocket permessage-deflate negotiation.

  • GHSA-vrm6-8vpv-qv8q / CVE-2026-1526 (High)
    Unbounded memory consumption in WebSocket permessage-deflate decompression.

Affected and patched ranges

  • CVE-2026-1525: affected 7.0.0 < 7.24.0, patched 7.24.0
  • CVE-2026-1528: affected 7.0.0 < 7.24.0, patched 7.24.0
  • CVE-2026-2581: affected >= 7.17.0 < 7.24.0, patched 7.24.0
  • CVE-2026-1527: affected 7.0.0 < 7.24.0, patched 7.24.0
  • CVE-2026-2229: affected 7.0.0 < 7.24.0, patched 7.24.0
  • CVE-2026-1526: affected 7.0.0 < 7.24.0, patched 7.24.0

title: fix(notebooks): plain paragraph on block split and trailing click
body:
## Problem

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->

<!-- Closes #ISSUE_ID -->

The notebook editor uses TipTap with a custom document schema 'heading block*' - the first node must be a heading, followed by any block nodes. Two ~~issues~~ papercuts with the current setup:

1. No trailing paragraph on click below content. StarterKit includes a TrailingNode extension that auto-appends a default block at the end of the document. But because our schema's `defaultBlockAt()` returns heading, not a paragraph. 
2. Splitting blocks preserves formatting. Pressing Enter mid-heading creates two headings. Pressing Enter mid-bold creates two bold blocks. The default splitBlock command preserves both the node type and marks.

![2026-04-01 10.04.02.gif](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/5924a0e7-a5da-4d3f-85f9-9a9b71ed389d.gif)

## Changes

1. Trailing paragraph on click (NotebookTrailingParagraph)

- Disables StarterKit's TrailingNode and adds a custom extension
- On mousedown below the last DOM element, inserts an empty paragraph and focuses it
- If an empty paragraph already exists at the end, just focuses it

1. Default paragraph on Enter (NotebookDefaultBlockOnEnter)

- After splitting, converts the new block to a paragraph if it inherited a non-paragraph type (e.g. heading)
- Selects the new block content and strips all marks (bold, italic, etc.)
- Skips this when pressing Enter at the start of a block (offset 0), so the original block keeps its type

![2026-04-01 10.06.49.gif](https://app.graphite.com/user-attachments/assets/c2dd2017-4cb3-46f9-a6cd-ce7a0e9c392d.gif)

title: fix(web-analytics): use $initial_current_url instead of $initial_url for channel type
body:
## Problem

The initial channel type URL matching was using `$initial_url` with a coalesce fallback, but `$initial_url` was never the correct property — the SDK sets `$initial_current_url`. The `$initial_url` fallback was introduced by mistake in #27226 and partially fixed with a coalesce in #50867.

## Changes

- Remove the `coalesce` fallback to `$initial_url` and use `$initial_current_url` directly
- Update tests to reflect the simplified behavior

## How did you test this code?

Updated existing tests. Could not run locally (no DB), but the change is minimal — replacing a coalesce with a direct property reference.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored with Claude Code. Used git history to trace the original introduction of `$initial_url` back to #27226, then simplified the coalesce added in #50867 to use `$initial_current_url` directly.

## Problem

When deleting an action, there's no indication of what resources depend on it. Insights, experiments, cohorts, and hog functions can all silently break. Users also cannot see which actions are used on the action detail page.

## Changes

- New endpoint: `GET /api/projects/:id/actions/:id/references/` returns a list of resources (insights, experiments, cohorts, hog functions) that reference the given action.

title: fix(flags): chunk ClickHouse queries to avoid exceeding max_bytes_to_read limit
body:
## Problem

PR #52816 switched `sync_feature_flag_last_called` from `events` to `events_recent`, but the task is still failing in prod-eu with `CHQueryErrorTooManyBytes` ("max bytes: 5.00 TiB"). Even `events_recent` is too large for a single query spanning the full 30-minute sync window in EU.

All 3 runs since the merge (22:30, 23:00, 23:30 UTC) have failed after exhausting retries.

## Changes

- Split the ClickHouse query into 5-minute time chunks (configurable via `FEATURE_FLAG_LAST_CALLED_AT_SYNC_CHUNK_MINUTES`) and merge results across chunks
- Cap lookback to `FEATURE_FLAG_LAST_CALLED_AT_SYNC_MAX_LOOKBACK_HOURS` (default 6h) to prevent excessive scanning when the checkpoint is stale
- Only retry on `CH_TRANSIENT_ERRORS` instead of all exceptions

## How did you test this code?

19 unit tests pass, including new tests for chunking behavior, chunk merging, max lookback capping, and transient error retries.

## Publish to changelog?

No

title: perf(max): use minimal serializer for conversation list, load full conversation on demand

labels.name: update-snapshots
labels.description: Enable auto commit snapshots for Storybook and Playwright

body:
## Summary

- The `/conversations/` list endpoint was serializing every conversation's full message history via langgraph state fetches, causing **8MB+ payloads and ~13s load times** just to render the conversation list sidebar
- Splits the concern of listing conversations from viewing them: the list only needs title, status, and a handful of metadata fields; the full message history is only needed when actually opening a conversation

## Changes

**Backend**
- `ConversationViewSet.list` now uses `ConversationMinimalSerializer` (no `messages`, no state fetch)
- `ConversationViewSet.retrieve` still uses `ConversationSerializer` (full detail including messages)

**Frontend**
- `api.conversations.list()` return type changed from `PaginatedResponse<ConversationDetail>` to `PaginatedResponse<Conversation>`
- `maxGlobalLogic.conversationHistory` is now `Conversation[]` — list metadata only, no messages
- `maxGlobalLogic` no longer has a `loadConversation` loader (that was fetching full detail on the global history store)
- `maxLogic` gains its own `loadConversation` loader that fetches a single `ConversationDetail` for the active conversation only
- `openConversation` action triggers `loadConversation` instead of looking up from history
- `mergeConversationList` helper replaces `mergeConversationHistory`/`mergeConversations` (the list no longer holds `ConversationDetail`)
- `maxThreadLogic` connects `loadConversation`/`loadConversationSuccess`/`setCurrentConversation` from `maxLogic` instead of `maxGlobalLogic`; `syncLoadedConversation` module-level helper handles both the load-complete and poll-complete paths without duplication

## Test plan

- [ ] Open Max sidebar — conversation list should load quickly (no message history fetched)
- [ ] Click a conversation — full thread loads correctly
- [ ] Open a conversation URL directly — conversation loads via `loadConversation`
- [ ] Start a new conversation, send a message — streams correctly, conversation appears in list on completion
- [ ] Cancel generation — conversation reloads correctly
- [ ] In-progress conversation (already streaming on another tab) reconnects on load
- [ ] Backend test: list endpoint does not return `messages` field
- [ ] Backend test: retrieve endpoint still returns `messages` field

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: feat(surveys): add multi-language translation support UI
body:
## Problem
Original (https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/45281)

So here's the situation: teams running global products need to collect feedback from users who speak different languages. Right now, our surveys only support one language per survey, which means they're stuck with some pretty bad options:

- **Create duplicate surveys for each language** - maintenance nightmare, fragmented data, and analytics get split across surveys (maintenance headaches too)
- **Use English-only surveys** - which tanks response rates from non-English speakers  

This PR implements the frontend UI for multi-language survey support. Now customers can add translations for any language they want, and the end users automatically see the correct version based on their language property. Much cleaner!

It's dependent on the backend support from https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/pull/45096

Closes #42154 

## Changes

### The Core Features

**1. Translations Management UI** (`SurveyTranslations.tsx`)

Added a new "Translations" tab/panel in the survey editor. We get:
- Language dropdown with 26 common languages pre-populated (but it accepts any language code we throw at it - custom keys)
- Full support for regional variants - think en-US vs en-GB, fr-FR vs fr-CA, es-ES vs es-MX
- Add/remove languages with confirmation dialogs (so you don't accidentally nuke someone's translation work)
- Click any language to enter translation edit mode

**2. Language Editing Mode** (`SurveyEdit.tsx`)

When editing a translation, there's a yellow sticky banner that makes it super clear which language we're working on. It says "Only user-facing text can be translated - all other fields are editable in the default language only" to prevent confusion.

We can quickly switch back to default language with an inline link.

**3. Comprehensive Pre-Save Validation** (`surveyLogic.tsx`)

This one's important for preventing nasty errors. Added a new `translationValidationErrors` selector that catches:

- **Empty strings**: These cause backend validation errors, so we block them entirely
- **Translation placeholders**: Fields still marked `[Translation needed]` need to be actually translated
- **Link URL security**: All link fields must start with `https://` or `mailto:` - this prevents XSS attacks via `javascript:` or `data:` URIs (keeping it in sync with backend's validation too)

The validation runs for both the default language and all translations. When there are issues, we get:
- Yellow collapsible warning banner (sticky, so it stays visible while you scroll)
- Errors grouped by language with clickable language names to jump directly to the problem
- Save button disabled with a clear tooltip explaining what needs fixing
- Banner auto-dismisses when you fix the issues (reactive validation FTW)

**4. Auto-Synchronization of Choices** (`SurveyEditQuestionRow.tsx`)

This was quite fun to solve too. When managing multiple choice or single choice questions:

- Add a choice in default language → All translations automatically get a `[Translation needed]` placeholder
- Remove a choice → It's deleted from all translations
- Reorder choices → Order syncs across all languages
- **We literally cannot create mismatched choice arrays** - it's impossible by design

This prevents a whole class of errors where choice counts don't match, which would break everything from frontend rendering to backend validation.

**5. Smart Banner Priority System** (`SurveyEdit.tsx`)

Only one banner shows at a time to avoid clutter:

1. **Priority 1**: Validation errors (critical - blocks save)
2. **Priority 2**: Language editing context (helpful guidance)

Both banners are sticky and stay visible while you scroll. The survey title/description scroll normally though - we don't want the entire header stuck to the top!

### UX Improvements

**Intelligent Placeholder Behavior**
- Empty translation fields show the default language text as a greyed-out placeholder - visual hint without actually saving placeholder data
- New choices auto-fill with `[Translation needed]` instead of empty strings (prevents save errors)
- Language switcher clearly shows "Default (Original)" so you always know where you are

**Translation Field Detection**
- Only user-facing text fields are editable when you're in translation mode
- Non-translatable fields are view-only
- Prevents customers from accidentally modifying settings that shouldn't be language-specific
- And probably more importantly, I think it helps customers work easier + declutter the mind (maybe?) when they know fully in their head they are only going to be updating user-facing content when the yellow sticky banner appears 

**Smooth Workflow**
- Adding a language auto-switches to that language for immediate translation
- Deleting a language returns to default view
- Validation errors let us click language names to quickly switch and fix

### Security Stuff

**XSS Prevention**
- Link field validation requires `https://` or `mailto:` prefix (matching backend's restriction/validation)
- Blocks `javascript:`, `data:`, and other sketchy URI schemes
- Validation enforced in both frontend and backend (defense in depth)
- All translation text is sanitized on render

**Data Integrity**
- Empty string validation prevents backend from crashing on missing required fields
- Choice synchronization prevents data structure mismatches
- Placeholder detection ensures incomplete translations don't accidentally ship

## Implementation Details

### Data Structure

```typescript
interface Survey {
  // Survey-level translations
  translations?: {
    [languageCode: string]: {
      name?: string
      description?: string
      thankYouMessageHeader?: string
      thankYouMessageDescription?: string
      thankYouMessageCloseButtonText?: string
    }
  }
  
  questions: Array<{
    // Question-level translations
    translations?: {
      [languageCode: string]: {
        question?: string
        description?: string
        buttonText?: string
        link?: string
        choices?: string[]
        lowerBoundLabel?: string
        upperBoundLabel?: string
      }
    }
  }>
}
```

### Key Selectors

**`translationValidationErrors`** - Does all the heavy lifting for validation:
- Scans all languages in survey and question translations
- Checks each translatable field against our validation rules
- Returns structured error array: `{ language, questionIndex, field, error }`
- Powers both the save button disable state and the validation banner

**`hasTranslationValidationErrors`** - Simple boolean gate:
- Derived from `translationValidationErrors.length > 0`
- Used to show/hide validation banner and disable save button

## Design Decisions (and why)

**Why sticky banners?**
Validation errors are critical - they should always be visible. The language context banner prevents confusion when you're scrolling through a long survey. We kept the scene title/description non-sticky though, so normal document flow still works.

**Why the banner priority system?**
Showing both banners at once creates visual clutter and increases cognitive load. Validation errors are more critical (they block save), so they take priority. When there are no errors, the language context banner shows to help you stay oriented.

**Why `[Translation needed]` instead of empty strings?**
Empty strings cause backend validation errors due to database constraints. Using a visible placeholder makes incomplete translations obvious, and our pre-save validation can detect and block them.

**Why auto-sync choices?**
This prevents a whole category of bugs where choice arrays have mismatched lengths between languages. It reduces cognitive load for translators (they don't have to manage array lengths), and makes it literally impossible to create invalid state.

**Why validate link format?**
Of course, security (and then the backend also blocks this as well). We need to prevent XSS attacks via `javascript:` or `data:` URIs. We allow `mailto:` for contact links though. The validation provides clear error messages to guide users to the correct format.

**Why disable non-translatable fields during translation mode?**
So for this, when editing a translation, we restrict customers to only editing user-facing text fields. Here's why:

*What could go wrong if we allowed it:*
- **Question type chaos**: Imagine changing a multiple choice question to a rating question while in French mode. Now we've got French translations with incompatible field structures (choices array vs rating labels).
- **Data integrity**: Adding/removing questions in translation mode would create orphaned translations or missing question mappings.
- **Better UX + Clarity**: Customers can easily keep track of the flow that they update survey's structure or add new changes in default language, then go back to translate the new content for other languages (and nothing more)

*What we allow instead:*
- Only user-facing text fields are editable: question text, description, choices, button labels, etc.
- All structural changes (question types,  adding/removing questions/choices etc) must be done in the default language
- The yellow banner makes this crystal clear: "Only user-facing text can be translated" and customers can instantly jump back to default language (without having to scroll down to the Translations tab/panel)

This prevents a whole category of bugs and keeps the survey structure consistent across all languages. When there's need to change structure, we switch back to default, make the change, and it applies to all languages. Then we go back and translate the new content. Feels like a kind of unidirectional flow that can't be easily mistaken

**Why validate empty choices in the default language?**
This one's subtle but important. When you add a new choice while translations exist, the system creates a placeholder `[Translation needed]` in all translations. But what if the user forgets to fill in the default choice itself?

*The problem:*
- User has French and Spanish translations active
- User adds a new choice to a multiple choice question
- User fills in the French translation: "Oui"
- User fills in the Spanish translation: "Sí"  
- User forgets to fill in the default (English) choice - it stays as empty string
- Backend throws validation error or prevents save because choices cannot be empty

*The fix:*
We also validate choices in the default language before checking translations. Empty string choices are never allowed - whether you're using translations or not. This ensures consistency:
- If a choice is empty in default → Validation error, save blocked
- If a choice is empty in translation → Validation error, save blocked
- If a choice has text in translation but empty in default → Validation error (because default needs a value)

**Why validate that default fields cannot be empty when translations exist (reverse validation)?**
This is the flip side of the coin - it prevents us from translating content that doesn't exist in the default language.

*The problem:*
- User creates a survey with a rating question
- User adds French translation
- User gets excited and fills in the French question text: "Que pensez-vous de notre produit?"
- User forgets to fill in the default (English) question text - leaves it empty
- Without reverse validation, this saves successfully
- But now you have a broken default language version - English end-users see no question text!
- Even worse, the survey might fail backend validation because required fields are empty

*The fix:*
When a default field is empty but translations have values, we show an error on the DEFAULT language: "Cannot be empty (has translation)". This makes it clear:
- You cannot translate something that doesn't exist in the source
- The default language is the source of truth - it must be complete first
- Fill in the default field, THEN translate it

*And then why do we  put the error on default instead of translation?*
Because the problem IS in the default! The translation itself is perfectly valid - the issue is that we're trying to translate from nothing. By putting the error on the default language, we guide customers to fix the root cause: fill in the default content first, then go translate it.

This enforces a healthy workflow: "translate FROM complete default" rather than allowing "translate TO without FROM". The default language must always be complete and valid, regardless of how many translations exist. We can think of it as protecting the integrity of our source content!

## How did you test this code

### Core workflow (Steps 1–3)
[surveys-translation-demo.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0cb9a82d-d5fc-4776-b716-20f35d0c6204)

This video demonstrates the core translation workflow:

**Step 1: Create base survey (default language)**
- Created "Product Satisfaction Q1 2026" survey with beta feedback template
- Added three questions: rating question (satisfied/not satisfied), multiple choice question (5 feature choices), and open text question with description
- Filled in thank you message (header, description, close button text)

**Step 2: Add French translation**
- Clicked Translations tab → added French (fr)
- Sticky yellow banner appeared: "Editing translation for French. Only user-facing text can be translated..."
- Language switcher showed "Default (Original)" | "French (fr)"
- Translatable fields showed default English text as placeholders
- Translated all content: survey name, description, all question fields, choices, thank you message
- Verified choices automatically showed default text as placeholders
- Confirmed non-translatable fields (question types and other 'structural' fields) were disabled

**Step 3: Add Spanish translation**
- Added custom language key (spanish-es) from language dropdown
- Translated all content to Spanish
- Demonstrated that custom language key (spanish-es) works

### Validation & UX polish

[survey-translations-validatinos-and-ux-polish-demo.mp4](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d6a0e002-8558-494a-b4ff-51536d0e8560)


**Validation features**
- Empty string validation: clear Question 1 text → "Cannot be empty" error on default
- Translation placeholder validation: leave `[Translation needed]` in French → validation error, collapsible banner, save disabled
- Link URL security: try `javascript:alert('xss')` → blocked with "Must start with https:// or mailto:" error
- Choice synchronization: add/remove choices in default → auto-sync with `[Translation needed]` placeholders in translations, deleted choices in default are reflected in other languages
- Reverse validation: empty default question but filled French translation → error on default: "Cannot be empty (has translation)"
- Banner priority: validation banner (yellow, sticky) takes priority over language editing banner

**Some UX polish features demoed**
- Reordering survey questions reflect in translations
- Smart field restrictions: question type dropdown disabled in translation mode
- Question type change cleanup: translations automatically clean up incompatible fields when question type changes. E.g from Multiple choice select → Link/Notification. Likewise, switching between similar structures Multiple choice select → Single choice select retains compatible fields like choices
- Language switcher clarity: highlighted active language or faulty one (in validation messages), e.g "Default (Original)" label, click to jump
- Intelligent placeholders: default text shown as greyed placeholders, new choices show `[Translation needed]`
- Sticky banner behavior: validation/language banners stick while scrolling, title/description scroll normally
- Delete confirmation: trash icon → confirmation dialog prevents accidental deletion

[surveys-translation-deletion-demo.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/66b3c17a-9610-41dd-a80d-4d1f2408515c)

## ⚠️ The Missing Piece - What's Left

Okay, so here's the thing - **this PR gets us 90% of the way there, but there's one critical piece missing**: the actual rendering logic in `posthog-js`.

Right now:
- ✅ Creators can add translations in PostHog UI
- ✅ Backend stores and validates translations
- ❌ **End users still see English** - the JS SDK doesn't know how to render translations yet!

**What needs to happen:**

The `posthog-js` SDK needs to:
1. Detect the user's language from person properties (e.g., `language: 'fr'`)
2. Merge translated fields with default survey fields
3. Render the translated version

Without this last piece, end users will always see the default language no matter what translations exist. So we'll need a companion PR in `posthog-js` to complete this epic! **(Good news: We have one already drafted here: [PostHog/posthog-js#3004](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog-js/pull/3004))**

## Changelog

Yes, I guess...

title: feat(data-warehouse): add cost_in_local_currency to LinkedIn Ads connector
body:
Resolves #52911

## Problem

The LinkedIn Ads data warehouse connector syncs `campaign_stats` and `campaign_group_stats` tables that include `cost_in_usd` and `conversion_value_in_local_currency`, but are missing `cost_in_local_currency`. The LinkedIn Marketing API's `/rest/adAnalytics` endpoint already returns `costInLocalCurrency` in the same response payload — it just isn't being extracted.

This means users who need cost data in their local currency (for non-USD accounts) have no way to get it from the connector without this field.

## Changes

- Added `costInLocalCurrency` to `FLOAT_FIELDS` in `schemas.py` so API values are converted to float
- Added `costInLocalCurrency` to `field_names` for both `CampaignStats` and `CampaignGroupStats` resource schemas
- Updated `test_flatten_float_fields` test to cover the new field
- Added `cost_in_local_currency` column to the marketing analytics test CSV

No changes needed to `linkedin_ads.py` (flattening logic) or `client.py` (API field list) — both derive behavior from the schema definitions automatically. DLT handles schema evolution for the new nullable column on next sync.

## How did you test this code?

- All 23 LinkedIn Ads unit tests pass (`pytest posthog/temporal/data_imports/sources/linkedin_ads/tests/ -v`)
- Lint passes (`ruff check` + `ruff format`)
- This is an agent-authored PR; manual end-to-end testing against the LinkedIn API has not been performed

## Publish to changelog?

no

## Docs update

No docs changes needed — this adds a new column to existing tables.

## 🤖 LLM context

This PR was co-authored by Claude Code (Opus 4.6). The change is minimal and additive — it follows the exact same pattern used for `costInUsd` and `conversionValueInLocalCurrency` which are already extracted.

title: Add expand/collapse for long planning and substeps content
body:
## Problem

Planning steps and action substeps can become very long, making the thread view cluttered and difficult to navigate on smaller screens. Users need a way to keep this content compact by default while still being able to view the full content when needed.

## Changes

- Added `MAX_COLLAPSED_CONTENT_HEIGHT` constant (240px) to keep planning and substeps content compact
- Modified `PlanningAnswer` component to:
  - Track expanded state and full-height state separately
  - Measure content height using a ref to detect when expansion is needed
  - Show expand/collapse button only when content exceeds the max height
  - Apply `max-h-60 overflow-y-auto` styling when collapsed
- Modified `AssistantActionComponent` to apply the same expand/collapse pattern to substeps
- Added `useRef` and `useEffect` imports to support content height measurement
- Both components now show an expand/collapse button with appropriate icons when content is truncated

## How did you test this code?

Code review of the implementation:
- Verified that refs are properly initialized and used for height measurement
- Confirmed that state management correctly tracks expanded/collapsed states
- Checked that the expand/collapse button only appears when `needsExpansion` is true
- Validated that the styling classes are correctly applied based on `isFullHeight` state
- Ensured the pattern is consistently applied to both `PlanningAnswer` and `AssistantActionComponent`

title: feat(replay): convert deprecated authorized domains to URL triggers

labels.name: update-snapshots
labels.description: Enable auto commit snapshots for Storybook and Playwright

body:
## Problem

Session replay's "authorized domains" setting is deprecated, but users with existing entries have no easy path to migrate to URL triggers. Writing regex by hand is tedious, especially with many domains.

## Changes

Adds a "Convert to URL triggers" button in the deprecated authorized domains section that opens a modal where users can:

- See each authorized domain mapped to a regex URL trigger pattern
- Edit the generated patterns before confirming
- Convert and remove: saves the new URL triggers and clears the deprecated authorized domains list in one step

Key implementation details:

- Custom string parser for domain entries instead of `new URL()` — the WHATWG URL parser rejects wildcard hostnames like `https://*.example.com`
- Generated regex uses `(?:[/?#:][\s\S]*)?$` suffix to match any path/query/hash/port after the host
- Port-based hosts are fully supported (explicit port in domain = exact port match; no port = port-agnostic)
- Captures a PostHog analytics event with full before/after snapshot for manual rollback if needed
- State managed via dedicated Kea logic (`replayAuthorizedDomainsMigrationModalLogic`)

## How did you test this code?

- Unit tests for domain-to-regex conversion with parameterized cases covering wildcards, ports, boundary safety
- RTL integration tests for the modal flow (render, edit, submit, API patch, analytics capture, validation errors)
- Storybook stories for visual review

## Publish to changelog?

No

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored with Cursor (Claude). All code changes were pair-programmed and reviewed.

title: fix(revenue-analytics): Persons materialized view joins
body:
## Problem
When querying `$virt_revenue` or `$virt_mrr` from `persons`, the revenue analytics lazy join crashes with `AttributeError: 'DataWarehouseTable' object has no attribute 'id'` when the `managed-viewsets` feature flag is ON and views are materialized.

The root cause: `_select_from_persons_revenue_analytics_table` assumed every view returned by `get_table()` is either a `RevenueAnalyticsBaseView` or a `SavedQuery`, but materialized views return a `DataWarehouseTable` (S3Table). The function tried to convert it via `_base_view_args_from_saved_query`, which accesses `.id` — a field `DataWarehouseTable` doesn't have.

This was not caught in the unit tests because we weren't materializing the saved queries for the tests with `managed-viewsets` flag on.

Related to https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/issues/52270
## Changes
**Fix materialized view handling in persons revenue analytics join** (`persons_revenue_analytics.py`):
- The view collection loop now works with any `Table` subclass — it only uses `name` and `fields`, which are available on all types
- Extracted code to helper functions (will reuse them when refactoring groups joins)

**Test infrastructure** (`test_persons_revenue_analytics.py`):
- Split `TestPersonsRevenueAnalytics` in two classes: one with `materialized-views` flag `True` and the other `False`
- Extracted `PersonsRevenueAnalyticsSetupMixin` to share setup across test classes
- Add setup functions to actually materialize the saved queries, making tests nearly identical to the real case

## How did you test this code?
All unit tests in `test_persons_revenue_analytics.py` passing; query snapshot changes reflecting that we're now reading from the materialized results (i.e. `.csv` file)
Manually: 
- set up revenue analytics locally with `managed-viewsets` flag on;
- wait for the sync to happen;
- create join between revenue analytics and persons tables
- go to SQL editor and query `SELECT $virt_mrr, $virt_revenue from persons` -> the query should work


## Publish to changelog?
no

## Docs update
no

## 🤖 LLM context

title: feat(batch-exports): add management command to backfill missing batch export runs
body:
## Problem

When batch export runs are missed (due to infrastructure problems, incidents, etc.), there's no tool to detect and backfill the gaps. We have a local script that we use as part of our runbook but this has some limitations such as support for custom schedule start times, timezones and weekly schedules.

## Changes

**New management command** (`posthog/management/commands/backfill_batch_export_runs.py`):
- `python manage.py backfill_batch_export_runs` finds gaps in batch export run history and triggers Temporal schedule backfills
- Supports `--lookback-hours` (default 48) or explicit `--start`/`--end` ISO-8601 date range
- Filters by `--batch-export-id` or `--destination-type`
- `--dry-run` mode to preview without triggering backfills
- Rejects paused/deleted exports with clear warnings

**Shared utility extraction** (`products/batch_exports/backend/service.py`):
- Moved `_align_timestamp_to_interval` from `backfill_batch_export.py` to `service.py` as `align_timestamp_to_interval` (public)
- Both the management command and the backfill workflow now use this shared function
- Fixes a bug where weekly interval alignment was anchored to start-of-day instead of start-of-week


## How did you test this code?

- [ ] Create automated tests

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

N/A

## 🤖 LLM context

Co-authored with Claude Code across an iterative session. Started from a local script, converted to management command, added tests, then refined through several rounds of review.

title: docs: Document `internal` parameter for sandboxed agent tasks
body:
## Changes

This PR updates the internal engineering documentation for sandboxed agents to document the new `internal` parameter added in #52779.

### Updated Files

- **`docs/published/handbook/engineering/ai/sandboxed-agents.md`** - Added the `internal` parameter to the `Task.create_and_run()` parameters table

### What's Documented

The `internal` parameter allows teams to create tasks that are hidden from the list endpoint by default:

- `internal` is a boolean field that defaults to `False`
- When `internal=True`, the task is excluded from list endpoint results by default
- Internal tasks can be retrieved by passing `internal=true` query parameter to the list endpoint
- Individual tasks can still be accessed by task ID regardless of the `internal` flag

---

Relates to #52779

title: perf(ci): scope semgrep-python to .py files and enable parallel jobs
body:
<!-- mendral:session:slack:TSS5W8YQZ:C0A7SH71ETY:1774964839.106039 -->
## Summary

- **`--include="*.py"`** — restricts all rules (including the 31 multilang rules from `p/owasp-top-ten`, `p/security-audit`, `p/trailofbits`) to Python files only. Non-Python files in `common/ ee/ posthog/ products/` (templates, TS, JSON, YAML) are already fully covered by `semgrep-general` and `semgrep-js`, so there is no security gap.
- **`--jobs auto`** — enables parallel scanning using all available CPUs on the runner (2 CPUs on `ubuntu-latest`).

## Motivation

`semgrep-python` has grown from ~255s → ~368s avg (+43%) since late January, driven by codebase growth and 819 rules scanning 6,959 files (only 5,037 of which are Python). These two flags together are expected to bring duration back toward the January baseline.

Insight: https://app.mendral.com/insights/01KN2366WK8WEPQ4KG326GMPVM

body:
## Problem

<!-- Who are we building for, what are their needs, why is this important? -->

<!-- Does this fix an issue? Uncomment the line below with the issue ID to automatically close it when merged -->

title: fix(llma): normalize nested token objects instead of dropping AI events
body:
## Problem

Closes #52887

A user reported that Bedrock model generations were not appearing in LLM analytics despite events being sent successfully (confirmed via SDK debug logs). The `validateAiEventTokensStep` in the ingestion pipeline was silently dropping `$ai_generation` events when token properties (`$ai_input_tokens`, `$ai_output_tokens`) contained nested objects instead of plain numbers. This happens when users on `@posthog/ai` < 7.3.0 use the Vercel AI SDK V3 with providers like Amazon Bedrock, which returns token usage as `{ total: 10585, noCache: 10585, cacheRead: 0 }`.

Events were dropped with only a debug-level log — no user-facing feedback at all. Users see events succeed in their SDK debug console but they never appear in LLM analytics.

Zendesk: https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/52751

## Changes

Two changes to `validateAiEventTokensStep`:

1. **Normalize nested token objects** — if a token property is an object with a numeric `total` field, extract that value instead of rejecting it.
2. **Never drop events for invalid token values** — instead of `drop()`, null out the invalid property, attach an ingestion warning, and return `ok()`. The event still flows through the pipeline and appears in LLM analytics (cost calculation handles null tokens gracefully).

## How did you test this code?

Unit tests updated and passing (847 tests). Tests cover:
- Valid values pass through unchanged
- Nested objects with `total` field are normalized to the numeric value
- Non-normalizable objects (no `total`, arrays) get nulled with a warning
- Edge cases like `{ total: NaN }` and `{ total: "not a number" }`
- Missing token properties are not added to the event
- Non-AI events are unaffected
- Integration test updated to verify events with invalid/nested tokens are processed (not dropped)

No publish to changelog

## Docs update

No docs changes needed.

title: feat: sign identification
body:
## Problem

Right now support widget relies on widget_session_id for ticket access control. Switching browsers/devices loses all tickets. The email-based restore flow is high-friction. This affects PostHog's own support sidebar AND any customer using the Conversations widget for their logged-in users.

## Changes

Add HMAC-based identity verification using distinct_id as a general Conversations product feature. Customers sign their user's distinct_id server-side with their team's secret_api_token. The widget API verifies the signature and uses distinct_id for ticket lookup instead of widget_session_id.

title: fix(marketing-analytics): divide Reddit Ads conversion values by 100
body:
https://us.posthog.com/project/2/support/tickets/201

Reddit Ads API returns CONVERSION*TOTAL_VALUE fields in cents. Without this division, conversion values display 100x too high.

Ref: https://ads-api.reddit.com/docs/v3/operations/Get%20A%20Report

## Problem

Reddit Ads conversion value fields (`conversion_purchase_total_value`, `conversion_signup_total_value`) were being displayed without unit conversion, showing values 100x higher than actual. For example, a $3,523 purchase value was displaying as $352,300.

The Reddit Ads API documentation states: "Divide by 100: CONVERSION*TOTAL_VALUE". The spend field was already correctly divided by 1,000,000 (microcurrency), but conversion values were missing the ÷100 conversion.

Reported by a customer who compared PostHog values against their Reddit Ads Manager dashboard.

## Changes

- **Backend adapter** (`reddit_ads.py`): Added `/ 100` to `conversion_purchase_total_value` and `conversion_signup_total_value` in `_get_reported_conversion_value_field()`
- **Frontend tiles** (`utils.ts`): Added `/ 100` to Reddit Ads `specialConversionLogic` for `ReportedConversionValue`, matching the pattern Pinterest uses for micro-dollars
- **Test data**: Updated Reddit Ads CSV fixture with non-zero conversion values to cover this case
- **Test assertion**: Added `total_conversion_value` check in `test_reddit_ads_adapter_with_real_data`

## How did you test this code?

- Updated test fixture CSV with known conversion values in cents
- Added assertion verifying correct dollar conversion (37,000 + 185,000 cents → $370 + $1,850 = $2,220)
- Could not run tests locally due to pre-existing env setup issue (HogQL parser `filter_expr` error), but changes are mechanical and follow existing patterns

## Publish to changelog?

no

title: chore(dashboards): add team_id to dashboard tile
body:
## Problem

  `DashboardTile` has no direct `team_id` column — it derives team membership indirectly through
  `dashboard.team_id`. HogQL system tables require a direct `team_id` field for automatic team-scoped
  filtering (the compiler injects `WHERE team_id = ?` on every query). Without it, `DashboardTile` cannot
  be exposed as a system table.

  ## Changes

  ### Model (`products/dashboards/backend/models/dashboard_tile.py`)
  - Add `team` ForeignKey to `DashboardTile` with `db_index=False` (index added concurrently in a later
  migration)
  - `save()` safety net auto-populates `team_id` from `dashboard.team_id`

  ### Migrations (safe, multi-step)
  - **0002** — Add nullable `team_id` column (`db_index=False` to avoid a blocking `CREATE INDEX`)
  - **0003** — Backfill `team_id` from parent dashboard in batches of 1,000
  - **0004** — Make `team_id` NOT NULL
  - **0005** — `AddIndexConcurrently` on `team_id` (non-blocking)

  ### Creation paths
  All 10 `DashboardTile` creation sites now pass `team_id` explicitly — important for `bulk_create` which
  bypasses `save()`:
  - `DashboardSerializer` (create, duplicate, update_or_create)
  - `InsightSerializer` (add tile to dashboard)
  - `UpsertDashboardTool` (AI dashboard creation)
  - `dashboard_templates.py` (insight, text, and button tiles)
  - `DashboardTile.copy_to_dashboard()`

  ### HogQL system table
  - Register `dashboard_tiles` in `system.py` with fields: `id`, `dashboard_id`, `insight_id`, `deleted`,
  `color`, `layouts`

  ### Other
  - `DashboardTileVisitor` (resource transfer) — include `team` in excluded fields
  - `DashboardTileSerializer` — include `team` in fields list

  ## How did you test this code?

  - Migration test verifies backfill populates `team_id` and the NOT NULL constraint holds
  - System table test verifies `dashboard_tiles` is queryable and team-scoped
  - Existing dashboard API tests and insight tests pass with snapshot updates

  ## Publish to changelog?

  No

  ## Docs update

  No frontend-facing changes.

title: RFC: PostHog AI Migration to Agents SDK Harness
body:
## Problem

PostHog AI ("Max") currently runs as a Python LangGraph agent tightly coupled within the Django process. This architecture limits execution capabilities, diverges from the sandbox-based approach used by PostHog Code, and creates maintenance challenges. We need a comprehensive migration plan to move Max to the @posthog/agent harness running in a sandbox, connected via MCP tools.

## Changes

Added a detailed RFC document (`ee/hogai/RFC_AGENTS_SDK_MIGRATION.md`) that outlines a complete migration strategy across 5 phases:

**Phase 0 (Foundation):**
- MCP infrastructure migration from Cloudflare Workers to in-house Node.js service
- MCP tool quality audit and gap analysis against existing MaxTools
- Latency optimization through Redis caching and regional routing

**Phase 1 (Backend):**
- Context engine refactoring to precalculate context in Django and inject as system_reminders
- Mode deprecation in favor of dynamic tool search
- Conversation history text injection for legacy conversations
- Memory system, plan mode, and subagent execution mapping
- Streaming adapter for SSE compatibility

**Phase 2 (Frontend):**
- Message format adaptation layer with feature flag
- Dual-path conversation management during transition

**Phase 3 (Evaluation):**
- Parallel eval harness for testing both old and new paths
- Regression detection and latency scoring

**Phase 4 (Latency Mitigation):**
- Pre-warming sandbox pools for online users
- Typing detection to start provisioning early
- Keep-alive for active sessions

**Phase 5 (Quality):**
- Prompt merging to maintain Max's personality and domain focus
- Quality baseline establishment and regression tracking

The RFC also documents:
- Detailed mapping of all 15+ MaxTools to MCP equivalents with identified gaps
- Recommended migration sequence with clear dependencies
- Rollout strategy with feature flags and gradual user rollout
- Intentionally removed features (memory onboarding, memory collectors, agent modes, LangGraph state machine)
- Open questions requiring further investigation
- Verification and testing approach

## How did you test this code?

N/A - This is a planning document (RFC) with no executable code changes. The document itself is a design artifact that will guide implementation in subsequent PRs.

## Publish to changelog?

No - This is an RFC/planning document, not a user-facing feature.

## Docs update

No docs update needed for this RFC. The RFC itself serves as the design documentation for the migration effort.

title: feat(marketing-analytics): add Integration health tab with UTM audit
body:
## Problem

Marketing analytics users have no way to verify that their ad platform campaigns are properly linked to UTM tracking in PostHog. Campaigns can be spending money without being tracked, and UTM events can arrive without matching any campaign — both leading to incomplete conversion goal attribution.

## Changes

### Backend (`products/marketing_analytics/backend/`)

- **UTM audit service** (`services/utm_audit.py`): Cross-references ad platform campaigns (with spend) against pageview events with UTM parameters to identify tracking gaps
  - Respects `campaign_field_preferences` (match by campaign name vs ID per integration)
  - Uses pre-computed lookup dicts for O(C+U) matching performance
  - Returns `MatchType` enum (auto/mapped/none) distinguishing direct matches from manual mappings
  - Includes `all_utm_events` with per-event `campaign_match` and `source_match` status
- **API endpoint** (`api.py`): GET `utm_audit` with `@validated_request`, proper DRF serializers with `help_text`, `dataclasses.asdict()` for serialization
- **Tests** (`services/test_utm_audit.py`): 49 tests covering cross-reference matching, field preferences, match type classification, `_load_team_mappings` edge cases, and `run_utm_audit` end-to-end

### Frontend (`frontend/src/scenes/`)

- **Integration health tab** on Marketing analytics scene (behind `MARKETING_ANALYTICS_UTM_AUDIT` feature flag)
  - **Campaigns tab**: Two-panel layout — ad platform campaigns (left) and UTM campaigns (right). Selecting a campaign reorders UTMs by string similarity (Levenshtein). "Map campaign" button opens the mapping modal with both values pre-filled
  - **Sources tab**: Single table of all UTM sources with Integration column, auto/mapped check icons, and actions menu
  - **UTM preferences tab**: Match field settings, custom source mappings, campaign name mappings
  - Summary stat cards (campaigns, not linked, UTM sources, unmapped sources)
  - Integration filter (single-select) that filters campaigns and settings
  - Independent from dashboard filters — always uses last 30 days, no date picker
  - Uses team's `baseCurrency` for spend formatting
- **Dynamic scene description** that changes per active tab
- **`initialCampaignName` prop** threaded through settings modal for pre-filling campaign mappings

## How did you test this code?

- 49 backend unit tests (pytest) covering all matching logic, field preferences, team mappings, and the `run_utm_audit` orchestrator
- TypeScript compilation verified (no errors)
- Ruff lint passing
- Feature is behind a feature flag (`MARKETING_ANALYTICS_UTM_AUDIT`) for safe rollout

## Publish to changelog?

no

title: fix(cohorts): fix static cohort duplication truncation and add admin re-sync
body:
## Problem

Customers duplicating large dynamic cohorts as static cohorts are seeing significant truncation — e.g., a 1.6M-member dynamic cohort producing a static copy with only 339K members (~21%).

Root cause investigation revealed three issues in the static cohort creation pipeline:

1. **`readonly=2` in HogQL settings** — `print_cohort_hogql_query` applied default `HogQLGlobalSettings` which includes `readonly=2`. This query gets embedded as a subquery inside an INSERT statement, and ClickHouse blocks writes under `readonly=2`.

2. **60s query timeout** — The default `max_execution_time=60` is too tight for large cohorts running as background Celery tasks on the `LONG_RUNNING` queue.

3. **Memory-heavy PG exclusion** — The PG insert path loaded all existing cohort member IDs into a Python set before excluding them, which could OOM or timeout on large cohorts.

Additionally, there was no way to re-trigger a failed static cohort sync without calling Celery tasks directly from a Django shell.

## Changes

- Set `readonly=0` and `max_execution_time=600` in `print_cohort_hogql_query` since the output is used inside INSERT statements in background tasks
- Replace the Python-side set exclusion with a SQL LEFT JOIN for PG inserts
- Add "Re-sync static cohort from source query" admin action to the Cohort admin
- Surface `is_static`, `count`, `is_calculating`, `errors_calculating` in the cohort admin list view

## How did you test this code?

- Existing cohort util tests updated and passing
- Cohort API tests passing (136 tests)
- Investigated the specific customer case (team 73073, cohorts 89076 → 92210) to confirm the diagnosis

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

N/A

title: feat(anomalies): add anomalies tab with Temporal train/score pipeline
body:
## Problem

Users have no way to passively discover anomalous metrics across their insights. Anomaly detection exists in PostHog but is opt-in per insight via alerts. There's no single place to answer "what's behaving unusually right now?" without configuring anything.

## Changes

Adds a new **Anomalies** tab to the Product Analytics page that runs anomaly detection on all active time-series insights out of the box and surfaces a ranked list of the most anomalous recent metrics.

**Backend:**
- `InsightAnomalyConfig` + `AnomalyScore` Django models with migration
- Temporal train/score pipeline with two schedules:
  - **Training** (hourly): discovers eligible insights, fits ensemble models (ZScore + KNN + PCA), serializes to S3
  - **Scoring** (every 5 min): loads pre-fitted models from S3, scores latest data points, writes AnomalyScore records
- `TrainableEnsemble` wrapper that splits train/score without modifying existing `BaseDetector` or the alert system
- S3 model storage via existing `posthog.storage.object_storage`
- Concurrency controlled via semaphore (5 training, 10 scoring concurrent child workflows)
- API: `GET /api/environments/:id/anomalies/` (list with filters), `POST .../exclude/`, `POST .../include/`

**Frontend:**
- New `Anomalies` tab between Insights and Alerts with BETA tag
- Searchable/filterable table with sparklines, color-coded score badges, insight links
- Window selector (24h/7d/30d), interval filter, text search
- Chart.js sparkline component with red dot on anomalous points

**Feature flagged:** `anomalies-tab` gates both frontend tab visibility and backend scoring per team. Runs on general-purpose Temporal queue for initial dogfooding.

## How did you test this code?

This is an agent-authored PR providing the initial scaffolding. Tests are TODO:
- `pytest posthog/temporal/tests/anomalies/` (Temporal workflow/activity tests)
- `pytest posthog/api/test/test_anomalies.py` (API endpoint tests)
- `pnpm --filter=@posthog/frontend jest Anomalies` (frontend component tests)

## Publish to changelog?

No -- feature is behind a feature flag for internal dogfooding first.

## Docs update

Not needed yet -- internal feature preview.

title: fix(insights): allow table scrolling in embedded contexts (like thread)

labels.name: update-snapshots
labels.description: Enable auto commit snapshots for Storybook and Playwright

body: ## Problem Insights tables displayed as table visualizations (e.g.trends with "Table" display) inside embedded contexts like the Max AI thread don't scroll vertically. The table content overflows its container but ScrollableShadows sets overflow-y: hidden, clipping the content. The outer h-96 scroll container in VisualizationArtifactAnswer can't scroll because the overflow is absorbed internally. bonus: `InsightVizDisplay__content__right` can be empty but take up space in DOM flow ## Changes - ScrollableShadows - Refactored to be data-attribute driven. direction is now optional — when omitted, both axes scroll an shadows appear independently on whichever axis overflows, using CSS custom properties toggled by Base UI's data-overflow- attributes. Removed constrainToDirection prop. - LemonTable - When allowContentScroll is true, omit direction (enabling both-axis scrolling) and add h-full overflow-hidden so the table respects its parent's height constraint - InsightVizDisplay - Pass embedded prop through to InsightsTable (was available but not forwarded) - InsightsTable - Enable allowContentScroll when embedded, no just on dashboards. ![2026-03-30 20 59 32](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ea8943a0-be65-4ebf-967e-d3a628e22045) > Just some other scrollable containers working as expected ![2026-03-30 21 13 10](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6d1ee3ba-98c6-45bc-b074-8029fbc76ddc) ## How did you test this code?
