title: feat(llma): bake aggregate metrics and sentiment into cluster events

labels.name: team/llm-analytics
labels.description: LLM Analytics

body:
## Summary

- Adds a new `compute_cluster_aggregates_activity` to the clustering workflow that computes per-cluster aggregate metrics (avg cost, latency, tokens, error rate, total cost) and sentiment
- The activity runs between labeling and event emission as a best-effort step — 5 min budget, 1 attempt, no retries. If it fails the workflow continues without metrics
- Sentiment is fetched by checking Redis cache first, then dispatching batched workflows to the sentiment worker queue (batches of 5, max 10 concurrent)
- Aggregated metrics including sentiment (label, score, per-label counts) are stored directly on each cluster in the `$ai_trace_clusters` / `$ai_generation_clusters` events
- New models: `ClusterSentiment`, `ClusterAggregateMetrics`, `ComputeAggregatesActivityInputs`
- `ClusterData` and `EmitEventsActivityInputs` gain an optional `metrics` / `cluster_metrics` field

This is PR 1/2 — backend only. Once this is deployed and runs for a few days, PR 2 will update the frontend to read from the baked metrics instead of making runtime HogQL queries.

## Test plan

- [ ] Unit tests for `_aggregate_operational_metrics` and `_aggregate_sentiment_per_cluster`
- [ ] Unit test for `fetch_item_metrics` data layer
- [ ] Verify activity registers and workflow runs end-to-end in dev
- [ ] Deploy and verify `$ai_trace_clusters` events contain `metrics` field on clusters
- [ ] Confirm workflow still completes successfully if sentiment worker is unavailable (graceful degradation)

title: fix(ci): extract common temporal worker path check and add migration …
body:
## Problem

Temporal workers run a `wait-for-migrations` init container that blocks until both Django and ClickHouse migrations are applied. Migration paths were not included in any temporal worker CD trigger, so migration-only changes (especially reverts) didn't trigger worker redeployments — leaving workers stuck on a stale image waiting for a migration that no longer exists.

Ref: https://github.com/PostHog/charts/pull/8696

## Changes

- Add `posthog/clickhouse/migrations/**` and `posthog/migrations/**` as common triggers for all temporal worker deployments
- Consolidate all temporal worker path detection into a single `dorny/paths-filter` step, replacing ~12 individual `git diff | grep` shell commands
- Extract shared paths (`posthog/temporal/common/**`, `start_temporal_worker.py`, `pyproject.toml`, `bin/temporal-django-worker`) into a `common_temporal` filter — previously duplicated in every worker's check
- Worker-specific paths preserved 1:1 from master — no paths dropped
- Filters ordered: `common_temporal`, `health_check`, then remaining workers alphabetically

## How did you test this code?

Line-by-line comparison of every worker's grep patterns on master vs the dorny filter paths — all worker-specific paths preserved exactly. No automated tests applicable for CI workflow changes.

## Publish to changelog?

No

## Docs update

N/A

title: feat(llma): add PostHog AI LLM analytics mode with Hog eval tool

labels.name: team/llm-analytics
labels.description: LLM Analytics

body:
## Problem

Users writing Hog evaluations have no in-product help for writing correct Hog code. There is also no PostHog AI mode that provides LLM-analytics-specific tools (searching traces, running eval tests) to assist with the evaluation authoring workflow.

Stacked on #49587.

## Changes

Adds a `LLMAnalytics` agent mode for PostHog AI. The mode auto-activates when navigating to any LLM analytics scene — no feature flag gate.

**Agent mode + tools**
- `AgentMode.LLMAnalytics` in `schema-assistant-messages.ts` and `posthog/schema.py`
- `ee/hogai/core/agent_modes/presets/llm_analytics.py`: mode preset registering `SearchLLMTracesTool` and `RunHogEvalTestTool`
- `RunHogEvalTestTool` (`products/llm_analytics/backend/tools/run_hog_eval_test.py`): compiles Hog source, queries recent `$ai_generation` / `$ai_metric` events, runs `run_hog_eval()` per event, returns verdict/reasoning/error — same helper as the Temporal activity

**Context injection**
- `MaxEvaluationContext` type and `MaxContextType.EVALUATION` enum value
- `evaluationToMaxContextPayload` and `MaxOpenContext.evaluation` in `utils.ts`
- `maxContextLogic.ts` updated with evaluation reducers, actions, and selectors
- Backend `context.py` renders `<evaluations_context>` block with evaluation state (name, type, Hog source) and conditionally injects the Hog language reference (only for `hog` type evals, not `llm_judge`)
- `prompts.py` adds `{{{ui_context_evaluations}}}` template variable and `HOG_EVALUATION_REFERENCE`

**Frontend**
- `max-constants.tsx`: mode definition with `run_hog_eval_test` tool entry, scenes set (all LLMA scenes)
- Mode auto-activates when navigating to any LLM analytics scene
- "Generate with AI" button in `EvaluationCodeEditor` (from parent PR #49587) opens Max side panel with evaluation context pre-loaded
- `templates.ts`: null-safe `ifNull()` guards on cost/latency Hog template

## How did you test this code?

Unit/integration tests included:

- `products/llm_analytics/backend/tools/test_run_hog_eval_test.py` — parameterized compilation success/error, no events, runtime error, result formatting, null returns, `$ai_metric` events
- `ee/hogai/context/test/test_context.py` — parameterized backend context rendering for evaluation state (hog with source, llm_judge without source, fallback names, STL reference)
- `frontend/src/scenes/max/maxContextConverters.test.ts` — evaluation context conversion (all fields, minimal fields, combined contexts)

## Publish to changelog?

No — this is part of an unreleased beta feature.
